```text
  2181→
  2182→	// Initialize analytics with the full CLI command (unless disabled)
  2183→	slog.Debug("Analytics initialization check", "noAnalytics", noAnalytics, "flag_should_disable", noAnalytics)
  2184→	if !noAnalytics {
  2185→		slog.Debug("Initializing analytics")
  2186→		fullCommand := strings.Join(os.Args, " ")
  2187→		if err := analytics.Init(fullCommand, version); err != nil {
  2188→			// Log error but don't fail - analytics should not break the app
  2189→			slog.Warn("Failed to initialize analytics", "error", err)
  2190→		}
  2191→		defer func() { _ = analytics.Close() }() // Analytics errors shouldn't break the app
  2192→	} else {
  2193→		slog.Debug("Analytics disabled by --no-usage-analytics flag")
  2194→	}
  2195→
  2196→	// Check for updates (blocking)
  2197→	utils.CheckForUpdates(version, noVersionCheck, silent)
  2198→
  2199→	// Ensure proper cleanup and logging on exit
  2200→	defer func() {
  2201→		if r := recover(); r != nil {
  2202→			slog.Error("=== SpecStory PANIC ===", "panic", r)
  2203→			// Still try to wait for cloud sync even on panic
  2204→			_ = cloud.Shutdown(cloud.CloudSyncTimeout)
  2205→			log.CloseLogger()
  2206→			panic(r) // Re-panic after logging
  2207→		}
  2208→		// Wait for cloud sync operations to complete before exiting
  2209→		cloudStats := cloud.Shutdown(cloud.CloudSyncTimeout)
  2210→
  2211→		// Track cloud sync analytics if we have stats
  2212→		if cloudStats != nil {
  2213→			// Calculate total attempted (all sessions that started sync)
  2214→			totalCloudSessions := cloudStats.SessionsAttempted
  2215→
  2216→			// Track cloud sync completion event
  2217→			if cloudStats.SessionsUpdated > 0 || cloudStats.SessionsCreated > 0 || cloudStats.SessionsErrored > 0 || cloudStats.SessionsTimedOut > 0 {
  2218→				analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCloudSyncComplete, analytics.Properties{
  2219→					"sessions_created":   cloudStats.SessionsCreated,
  2220→					"sessions_updated":   cloudStats.SessionsUpdated,
  2221→					"sessions_skipped":   cloudStats.SessionsSkipped,
  2222→					"sessions_errored":   cloudStats.SessionsErrored,
  2223→					"sessions_timed_out": cloudStats.SessionsTimedOut,
  2224→					"total":              totalCloudSessions,
  2225→				})
  2226→			}
  2227→
  2228→			// Display cloud sync stats if not in silent mode
  2229→			if !silent && totalCloudSessions > 0 {
  2230→				fmt.Println() // Visual separation from provider sync output
  2231→
  2232→				// Determine if sync was complete or incomplete based on errors/timeouts
  2233→				if cloudStats.SessionsErrored > 0 || cloudStats.SessionsTimedOut > 0 {
  2234→					fmt.Printf("❌ Cloud sync incomplete! 📊 %s%d%s %s processed\n",
  2235→						log.ColorBoldCyan, totalCloudSessions, log.ColorReset, pluralSession(int(totalCloudSessions)))
  2236→				} else {
  2237→					fmt.Printf("☁️  Cloud sync complete! 📊 %s%d%s %s processed\n",
  2238→						log.ColorBoldCyan, totalCloudSessions, log.ColorReset, pluralSession(int(totalCloudSessions)))
  2239→				}
  2240→				fmt.Println()
  2241→
  2242→				fmt.Printf("  ⏭️ %s%d %s up to date (skipped)%s\n",
  2243→					log.ColorCyan, cloudStats.SessionsSkipped, pluralSession(int(cloudStats.SessionsSkipped)), log.ColorReset)
  2244→				fmt.Printf("  ♻️ %s%d %s updated%s\n",
  2245→					log.ColorYellow, cloudStats.SessionsUpdated, pluralSession(int(cloudStats.SessionsUpdated)), log.ColorReset)
  2246→				fmt.Printf("  ✨ %s%d new %s created%s\n",
  2247→					log.ColorGreen, cloudStats.SessionsCreated, pluralSession(int(cloudStats.SessionsCreated)), log.ColorReset)
  2248→
  2249→				// Only show errors if there were any
  2250→				if cloudStats.SessionsErrored > 0 {
  2251→					fmt.Printf("  ❌ %s%d %s errored%s\n",
  2252→						log.ColorRed, cloudStats.SessionsErrored, pluralSession(int(cloudStats.SessionsErrored)), log.ColorReset)
  2253→				}
  2254→
  2255→				// Only show timed out sessions if there were any
  2256→				if cloudStats.SessionsTimedOut > 0 {
  2257→					fmt.Printf("  ⏱️  %s%d %s timed out%s\n",
  2258→						log.ColorRed, cloudStats.SessionsTimedOut, pluralSession(int(cloudStats.SessionsTimedOut)), log.ColorReset)
  2259→				}
  2260→				fmt.Println()
  2261→
  2262→				// Display link to SpecStory Cloud (deep link to session if from run command)
  2263→				cwd, cwdErr := os.Getwd()
  2264→				if cwdErr == nil {
  2265→					identityManager := utils.NewProjectIdentityManager(cwd)
  2266→					if projectID, err := identityManager.GetProjectID(); err == nil {
  2267→						fmt.Printf("💡 Search and chat with your AI conversation history at:\n")
  2268→						if lastRunSessionID != "" {
  2269→							// Deep link to the specific session from run command
  2270→							fmt.Printf("   %shttps://cloud.specstory.com/projects/%s/sessions/%s%s\n\n",
  2271→								log.ColorBoldCyan, projectID, lastRunSessionID, log.ColorReset)
  2272→						} else {
  2273→							// Link to project overview for sync command
  2274→							fmt.Printf("   %shttps://cloud.specstory.com/projects/%s%s\n\n",
  2275→								log.ColorBoldCyan, projectID, log.ColorReset)
  2276→						}
  2277→					}
  2278→				}
  2279→			}
  2280→		}
  2281→
  2282→		if console || logFile {
  2283→			slog.Info("=== SpecStory Exiting ===", "code", 0, "status", "normal termination")
  2284→		}
  2285→		log.CloseLogger()
  2286→	}()
  2287→
  2288→	if err := fang.Execute(context.Background(), rootCmd, fang.WithVersion(version)); err != nil {
  2289→		// Check if we're running the check command by looking at the executed command
  2290→		executedCmd, _, _ := rootCmd.Find(os.Args[1:])
  2291→		if executedCmd == checkCmd {
  2292→			if console || logFile {
  2293→				slog.Error("=== SpecStory Exiting ===", "code", 2, "status", "agent execution failure")
  2294→				slog.Error("Error", "error", err)
  2295→			}
  2296→			// For check command, the error details are handled by checkSingleProvider/checkAllProviders
  2297→			// So we just exit silently here
  2298→			_ = cloud.Shutdown(cloud.CloudSyncTimeout)
  2299→			os.Exit(2)
  2300→		} else {
  2301→			if console || logFile {
  2302→				slog.Error("=== SpecStory Exiting ===", "code", 1, "status", "error")
  2303→				slog.Error("Error", "error", err)
  2304→			}
  2305→			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr) // Visual separation makes error output more noticeable
  2306→
  2307→			// Only show usage for actual command/flag errors from Cobra
  2308→			// These are errors like "unknown command", "unknown flag", "invalid argument", etc.
  2309→			// For all other errors (authentication, network, file system, etc.), we should NOT show usage
  2310→			errMsg := err.Error()
  2311→			isCommandError := strings.Contains(errMsg, "unknown command") ||
  2312→				strings.Contains(errMsg, "unknown flag") ||
  2313→				strings.Contains(errMsg, "invalid argument") ||
  2314→				strings.Contains(errMsg, "required flag") ||
  2315→				strings.Contains(errMsg, "accepts") || // e.g., "accepts 1 arg(s), received 2"
  2316→				strings.Contains(errMsg, "no such flag") ||
  2317→				strings.Contains(errMsg, "flag needs an argument")
  2318→
  2319→			if isCommandError {
  2320→				_ = rootCmd.Usage() // Ignore error; we're exiting anyway
  2321→				fmt.Println()       // Add visual separation after usage for better CLI readability
  2322→			}
  2323→			_ = cloud.Shutdown(cloud.CloudSyncTimeout)
  2324→			os.Exit(1)
  2325→		}
  2326→	}
  2327→}
  2328→
```

 `./pkg/spi/schema/types.go`

```text
     1→package schema
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"log/slog"
     5→)
     6→
     7→// Role constants for message types
     8→const (
     9→	RoleUser  = "user"
    10→	RoleAgent = "agent"
    11→)
    12→
    13→// ContentType constants for content parts
    14→const (
    15→	ContentTypeText     = "text"
    16→	ContentTypeThinking = "thinking"
    17→)
    18→
    19→// ToolType constants for tool classification
    20→const (
    21→	ToolTypeWrite   = "write"
    22→	ToolTypeRead    = "read"
    23→	ToolTypeSearch  = "search"
    24→	ToolTypeShell   = "shell"
    25→	ToolTypeTask    = "task"
    26→	ToolTypeGeneric = "generic"
    27→	ToolTypeUnknown = "unknown"
    28→)
    29→
    30→// SessionData is the unified data format for sessions from all terminal coding agent providers
    31→type SessionData struct {
    32→	SchemaVersion string       `json:"schemaVersion"`
    33→	Provider      ProviderInfo `json:"provider"`
    34→	SessionID     string       `json:"sessionId"`
    35→	CreatedAt     string       `json:"createdAt"`
    36→	UpdatedAt     string       `json:"updatedAt,omitempty"`
    37→	Slug          string       `json:"slug,omitempty"`
    38→	WorkspaceRoot string       `json:"workspaceRoot"`
    39→	Exchanges     []Exchange   `json:"exchanges"`
    40→}
    41→
    42→// ProviderInfo is the information about the agent provider that created the session
    43→type ProviderInfo struct {
    44→	ID      string `json:"id"`
    45→	Name    string `json:"name"`
    46→	Version string `json:"version"`
    47→}
    48→
    49→// Exchange is a conversational turn with ordered messages
    50→type Exchange struct {
    51→	ExchangeID string                 `json:"exchangeId"`
    52→	StartTime  string                 `json:"startTime,omitempty"`
    53→	EndTime    string                 `json:"endTime,omitempty"`
    54→	Messages   []Message              `json:"messages"`
    55→	Metadata   map[string]interface{} `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
    56→}
    57→
    58→// Message is either a user or agent message
    59→type Message struct {
    60→	ID        string                 `json:"id,omitempty"`
    61→	Timestamp string                 `json:"timestamp,omitempty"`
    62→	Role      string                 `json:"role"` // "user" or "agent"
    63→	Model     string                 `json:"model,omitempty"`
    64→	Content   []ContentPart          `json:"content,omitempty"`
    65→	Tool      *ToolInfo              `json:"tool,omitempty"`
    66→	PathHints []string               `json:"pathHints,omitempty"`
    67→	Metadata  map[string]interface{} `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
    68→}
    69→
    70→// ContentPart is a content part (text or thinking)
    71→type ContentPart struct {
    72→	Type string `json:"type"` // "text" or "thinking"
    73→	Text string `json:"text"` // Content text (for both text and thinking)
    74→}
    75→
    76→// ToolInfo is tool use information
    77→type ToolInfo struct {
    78→	Name string `json:"name"` // Name of the tool
    79→
    80→	// Tool type classification:
    81→	// Use `write` for tools that write to the file system
    82→	// Use `read` for tools that read from the file system
    83→	// Use `search` for tools that search the file system or the web (e.g. `grep`, `glob`, `find`, `web_search` etc.)
    84→	// Use `shell` for tools that run shell commands (e.g. `bash`, `sed`, `awk`, `ls` etc.)
    85→	// Use `task` for tools that manage tasks (e.g. `todo`, `todo_write`, `todo_read`, `tasklist`, `task_manager` etc.)
    86→	// Use `generic` for tools that the provider knows about but that don't map well to a specific type
    87→	// Use `unknown` for tools that the provider does not know about
    88→	Type string `json:"type"` // Type of the tool: write, read, search, shell, task, generic, unknown
    89→
    90→	UseID             string                 `json:"useId,omitempty"`             // ID of the tool use
    91→	Input             map[string]interface{} `json:"input,omitempty"`             // Input to the tool
    92→	Output            map[string]interface{} `json:"output,omitempty"`            // Output from the tool
    93→	Summary           *string                `json:"summary,omitempty"`           // Summary content (will be wrapped in <summary> tags by CLI)
    94→	FormattedMarkdown *string                `json:"formattedMarkdown,omitempty"` // Pre-formatted markdown from provider (will be wrapped in <formatted-markdown> tags by CLI)
    95→}
    96→
    97→// Validate checks the SessionData against schema constraints and logs warnings for any issues.
    98→// Returns true if validation passed, false if there were any issues.
    99→func (s *SessionData) Validate() bool {
   100→	valid := true
   101→
   102→	// Check schema version
   103→	if s.SchemaVersion != "1.0" {
   104→		slog.Warn("schema validation: schemaVersion must be '1.0'", "got", s.SchemaVersion)
   105→		valid = false
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	// Check required root fields
   109→	if s.Provider.ID == "" {
   110→		slog.Warn("schema validation: provider.id is required")
   111→		valid = false
   112→	}
   113→	if s.Provider.Name == "" {
   114→		slog.Warn("schema validation: provider.name is required")
   115→		valid = false
   116→	}
   117→	if s.Provider.Version == "" {
   118→		slog.Warn("schema validation: provider.version is required")
   119→		valid = false
   120→	}
   121→	if s.SessionID == "" {
   122→		slog.Warn("schema validation: sessionId is required")
   123→		valid = false
   124→	}
   125→	if s.CreatedAt == "" {
   126→		slog.Warn("schema validation: createdAt is required")
   127→		valid = false
   128→	}
   129→	if s.WorkspaceRoot == "" {
   130→		slog.Warn("schema validation: workspaceRoot is required")
   131→		valid = false
   132→	}
   133→
   134→	// Validate each exchange
   135→	for i, exchange := range s.Exchanges {
   136→		if exchange.ExchangeID == "" {
   137→			slog.Warn("schema validation: exchange.exchangeId is required", "exchangeIndex", i)
   138→			valid = false
   139→		}
   140→
   141→		// Validate each message in the exchange
   142→		for j, msg := range exchange.Messages {
   143→			if !msg.validate(i, j) {
   144→				valid = false
   145→			}
   146→		}
   147→	}
   148→
   149→	return valid
   150→}
   151→
   152→// validate checks a single message against schema constraints.
   153→func (m *Message) validate(exchangeIndex, messageIndex int) bool {
   154→	valid := true
   155→	logCtx := []any{"exchangeIndex", exchangeIndex, "messageIndex", messageIndex}
   156→
   157→	// Check role is valid
   158→	if m.Role != RoleUser && m.Role != RoleAgent {
   159→		slog.Warn("schema validation: message.role must be 'user' or 'agent'",
   160→			append(logCtx, "got", m.Role)...)
   161→		valid = false
   162→	}
   163→
   164→	// User message constraints
   165→	if m.Role == RoleUser {
   166→		if len(m.Content) == 0 {
   167→			slog.Warn("schema validation: user message must have non-empty content", logCtx...)
   168→			valid = false
   169→		}
   170→		if m.Tool != nil {
   171→			slog.Warn("schema validation: user message cannot have tool", logCtx...)
   172→			valid = false
   173→		}
   174→		if m.Model != "" {
   175→			slog.Warn("schema validation: user message cannot have model", logCtx...)
   176→			valid = false
   177→		}
   178→	}
   179→
   180→	// Agent message constraints: must have at least one of content, tool, or pathHints
   181→	if m.Role == RoleAgent {
   182→		hasContent := len(m.Content) > 0
   183→		hasTool := m.Tool != nil
   184→		hasPathHints := len(m.PathHints) > 0
   185→		if !hasContent && !hasTool && !hasPathHints {
   186→			slog.Warn("schema validation: agent message must have content, tool, or pathHints", logCtx...)
   187→			valid = false
   188→		}
   189→	}
   190→
   191→	// Validate content parts
   192→	for k, part := range m.Content {
   193→		if !part.validate(exchangeIndex, messageIndex, k) {
   194→			valid = false
   195→		}
   196→	}
   197→
   198→	// Validate tool if present
   199→	if m.Tool != nil {
   200→		if !m.Tool.validate(exchangeIndex, messageIndex) {
   201→			valid = false
   202→		}
   203→	}
   204→
   205→	return valid
   206→}
   207→
   208→// validate checks a single content part against schema constraints.
   209→func (c *ContentPart) validate(exchangeIndex, messageIndex, partIndex int) bool {
   210→	valid := true
   211→	logCtx := []any{"exchangeIndex", exchangeIndex, "messageIndex", messageIndex, "partIndex", partIndex}
   212→
   213→	if c.Type != ContentTypeText && c.Type != ContentTypeThinking {
   214→		slog.Warn("schema validation: contentPart.type must be 'text' or 'thinking'",
   215→			append(logCtx, "got", c.Type)...)
   216→		valid = false
   217→	}
   218→
   219→	return valid
   220→}
   221→
   222→// validate checks tool info against schema constraints.
   223→func (t *ToolInfo) validate(exchangeIndex, messageIndex int) bool {
   224→	valid := true
   225→	logCtx := []any{"exchangeIndex", exchangeIndex, "messageIndex", messageIndex}
   226→
   227→	if t.Name == "" {
   228→		slog.Warn("schema validation: tool.name is required", logCtx...)
   229→		valid = false
   230→	}
   231→
   232→	validTypes := map[string]bool{
   233→		ToolTypeWrite:   true,
   234→		ToolTypeRead:    true,
   235→		ToolTypeSearch:  true,
   236→		ToolTypeShell:   true,
   237→		ToolTypeTask:    true,
   238→		ToolTypeGeneric: true,
   239→		ToolTypeUnknown: true,
   240→	}
   241→	if !validTypes[t.Type] {
   242→		slog.Warn("schema validation: tool.type must be one of: write, read, search, shell, task, generic, unknown",
   243→			append(logCtx, "got", t.Type)...)
   244→		valid = false
   245→	}
   246→
   247→	return valid
   248→}
   249→
```

```text
     1→package spi
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→
     6→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
     7→)
     8→
     9→// CheckResult contains the result of a provider check operation
    10→type CheckResult struct {
    11→	Success      bool   // Whether the check succeeded
    12→	Version      string // Version of the provider (empty on failure)
    13→	Location     string // File path/location of the provider executable
    14→	ErrorMessage string // Error message if check failed (empty on success)
    15→}
    16→
    17→// ProgressCallback reports progress during session parsing/processing
    18→// current: number of items processed so far (1-based)
    19→// total: total number of items to process
    20→type ProgressCallback func(current, total int)
    21→
    22→// AgentChatSession represents a chat session from an AI coding agent
    23→type AgentChatSession struct {
    24→	SessionID   string              // Stable and unique identifier for the session (often a UUID)
    25→	CreatedAt   string              // Stable ISO 8601 timestamp when session was created
    26→	Slug        string              // Stable human-readable but file name safe slug, often derived from first user message
    27→	SessionData *schema.SessionData // Structured session data in unified format
    28→	RawData     string              // Raw session data (e.g., JSON blobs for Cursor CLI, JSONL for Claude Code and Codex CLI, etc.)
    29→}
    30→
    31→// SessionMetadata contains lightweight metadata about a session without full content
    32→// Used by ListAgentChatSessions for efficient session listing
    33→type SessionMetadata struct {
    34→	SessionID string `json:"session_id" csv:"session_id"` // Stable and unique identifier for the session
    35→	CreatedAt string `json:"created_at" csv:"created_at"` // Stable ISO 8601 timestamp when session was created
    36→	Slug      string `json:"slug" csv:"slug"`             // Stable human-readable session name/slug
    37→	Name      string `json:"name" csv:"name"`             // Human-readable description of the session (may be empty if not available)
    38→}
    39→
    40→// Provider defines the interface that all agent coding tool providers must implement
    41→type Provider interface {
    42→	// Name returns the human-readable name of the provider (e.g., "Claude Code", "Cursor CLI", "Codex CLI")
    43→	Name() string
    44→
    45→	// Check verifies if the provider is properly installed and returns version info
    46→	// customCommand: empty string = use detected/default binary path, non-empty = use this specific command/path
    47→	Check(customCommand string) CheckResult
    48→
    49→	// DetectAgent checks if the provider's agent has been used in the given project path
    50→	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
    51→	// helpOutput: if true AND no activity is found, the provider should output helpful guidance
    52→	// Returns true if the agent has created artifacts/sessions in the specified path
    53→	DetectAgent(projectPath string, helpOutput bool) bool
    54→
    55→	// GetAgentChatSession retrieves a single chat session by ID for the given project path
    56→	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
    57→	// sessionID: specific session identifier to retrieve (always provided, never empty)
    58→	// debugRaw: if true, provider should write provider-specific raw debug files to .specstory/debug/<sessionID>/
    59→	//           (e.g., numbered JSON files). The unified session-data.json is written centrally by the CLI.
    60→	// Returns nil if the session is not found, error for actual errors
    61→	GetAgentChatSession(projectPath string, sessionID string, debugRaw bool) (*AgentChatSession, error)
    62→
    63→	// GetAgentChatSessions retrieves all chat sessions for the given project path
    64→	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
    65→	// debugRaw: if true, provider should write provider-specific raw debug files to .specstory/debug/<sessionID>/
    66→	//           (e.g., numbered JSON files). The unified session-data.json is written centrally by the CLI.
    67→	// progress: optional callback for reporting progress during parsing (nil = no progress reporting)
    68→	// Returns a slice of AgentChatSession structs containing session data
    69→	GetAgentChatSessions(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, progress ProgressCallback) ([]AgentChatSession, error)
    70→
    71→	// ListAgentChatSessions retrieves lightweight metadata for all sessions without full parsing
    72→	// This should be faster than GetAgentChatSessions as it only needs to return SessionMetadata and not the full AgentChatSession
    73→	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
    74→	// Returns a slice of SessionMetadata (ordering is provider-defined; consumers will sort if needed)
    75→	ListAgentChatSessions(projectPath string) ([]SessionMetadata, error)
    76→
    77→	// ExecAgentAndWatch executes the agent in interactive mode and watches for session updates
    78→	// Blocks until the agent exits, calling sessionCallback for each new/updated session
    79→	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
    80→	// customCommand: empty string = use detected/default binary with default args, non-empty = use this specific command/path
    81→	// resumeSessionID: empty string = start new session, non-empty = resume this specific session ID
    82→	// debugRaw: if true, provider should write provider-specific raw debug files to .specstory/debug/<sessionID>/
    83→	//           (e.g., numbered JSON files). The unified session-data.json is written centrally by the CLI.
    84→	// sessionCallback is called with each session update (provider should not block on callback)
    85→	// The implementation should handle its own file watching and session tracking
    86→	ExecAgentAndWatch(projectPath string, customCommand string, resumeSessionID string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*AgentChatSession)) error
    87→
    88→	// WatchAgent watches for agent activity and calls the callback with an updated AgentChatSession
    89→	// Does NOT execute the agent - only watches for new activity
    90→	// Runs until error or context cancellation
    91→	// ctx: Context for cancellation and timeout control
    92→	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
    93→	// debugRaw: if true, provider should write provider-specific raw debug files to .specstory/debug/<sessionID>/
    94→	//           (e.g., numbered JSON files). The unified session-data.json is written centrally by the CLI.
    95→	// sessionCallback: called with AgentChatSession on each update (provider should not block on callback)
    96→	// The implementation should handle its own file watching and session tracking
    97→	WatchAgent(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*AgentChatSession)) error
    98→}
    99→
```

 `./pkg/spi/factory/registry.go`

```text
     1→// Package factory provides the provider registry and factory implementation.
     2→// This package imports all concrete provider implementations and manages
     3→// their registration, avoiding circular dependencies in the SPI layer.
     4→package factory
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"log/slog"
     9→	"sort"
    10→	"strings"
    11→	"sync"
    12→
    13→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/claudecode"
    14→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/codexcli"
    15→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli"
    16→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/droidcli"
    17→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli"
    18→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
    19→)
    20→
    21→// Registry manages all registered providers
    22→type Registry struct {
    23→	providers         map[string]spi.Provider // key is the provider ID (e.g., "claude", "cursor")
    24→	mu                sync.RWMutex
    25→	initialized       bool
    26→	providerListCache string    // Cached formatted provider list string
    27→	providerListOnce  sync.Once // Ensures provider list is built only once
    28→}
    29→
    30→// Global registry instance
    31→var (
    32→	registry = &Registry{
    33→		providers: make(map[string]spi.Provider),
    34→	}
    35→	once sync.Once
    36→)
    37→
    38→// ensureInitialized makes sure providers are registered
    39→func (r *Registry) ensureInitialized() {
    40→	once.Do(func() {
    41→		r.registerAll()
    42→	})
    43→}
    44→
    45→// registerAll registers all known providers.
    46→// This is the ONLY place that needs to be updated when adding new providers.
    47→func (r *Registry) registerAll() {
    48→	r.mu.Lock()
    49→	defer r.mu.Unlock()
    50→
    51→	if r.initialized {
    52→		slog.Debug("Provider registry already initialized, skipping registration")
    53→		return
    54→	}
    55→
    56→	slog.Debug("Initializing provider registry")
    57→
    58→	// Register providers with simple IDs
    59→	// Each provider directly implements the spi.Provider interface
    60→	claudeProvider := claudecode.NewProvider()
    61→	r.providers["claude"] = claudeProvider
    62→	slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "claude", "name", claudeProvider.Name())
    63→
    64→	cursorProvider := cursorcli.NewProvider()
    65→	r.providers["cursor"] = cursorProvider
    66→	slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "cursor", "name", cursorProvider.Name())
    67→
    68→	codexProvider := codexcli.NewProvider()
    69→	r.providers["codex"] = codexProvider
    70→	slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "codex", "name", codexProvider.Name())
    71→
    72→	geminiProvider := geminicli.NewProvider()
    73→	r.providers["gemini"] = geminiProvider
    74→	slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "gemini", "name", geminiProvider.Name())
    75→
    76→	droidProvider := droidcli.NewProvider()
    77→	r.providers["droid"] = droidProvider
    78→	slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "droid", "name", droidProvider.Name())
    79→
    80→	r.initialized = true
    81→	slog.Info("Provider registry initialized", "count", len(r.providers), "providers", r.ListIDsUnsafe())
    82→}
    83→
    84→// ListIDsUnsafe returns provider IDs without locking (for internal use only)
    85→func (r *Registry) ListIDsUnsafe() []string {
    86→	ids := make([]string, 0, len(r.providers))
    87→	for id := range r.providers {
    88→		ids = append(ids, id)
    89→	}
    90→	sort.Strings(ids)
    91→	return ids
    92→}
    93→
    94→// GetRegistry returns the global registry instance
    95→func GetRegistry() *Registry {
    96→	registry.ensureInitialized()
    97→	return registry
    98→}
    99→
   100→// Get retrieves a provider by ID (case-insensitive)
   101→func (r *Registry) Get(id string) (spi.Provider, error) {
   102→	r.ensureInitialized()
   103→	r.mu.RLock()
   104→	defer r.mu.RUnlock()
   105→
   106→	// Case-insensitive lookup
   107→	for provID, provider := range r.providers {
   108→		if strings.EqualFold(provID, id) {
   109→			slog.Debug("Retrieved provider", "requested_id", id, "matched_id", provID, "name", provider.Name())
   110→			return provider, nil
   111→		}
   112→	}
   113→
   114→	// Log available providers when lookup fails to aid debugging
   115→	availableIDs := make([]string, 0, len(r.providers))
   116→	for id := range r.providers {
   117→		availableIDs = append(availableIDs, id)
   118→	}
   119→	sort.Strings(availableIDs)
   120→
   121→	slog.Warn("Provider not found",
   122→		"requested_id", id,
   123→		"available_providers", availableIDs)
   124→
   125→	return nil, fmt.Errorf("provider '%s' not found", id)
   126→}
   127→
   128→// GetAll returns all registered providers as a map of ID to Provider
   129→func (r *Registry) GetAll() map[string]spi.Provider {
   130→	r.ensureInitialized()
   131→	r.mu.RLock()
   132→	defer r.mu.RUnlock()
   133→
   134→	// Return a copy to prevent external modification
   135→	result := make(map[string]spi.Provider)
   136→	for id, provider := range r.providers {
   137→		result[id] = provider
   138→	}
   139→
   140→	return result
   141→}
   142→
   143→// ListIDs returns all registered provider IDs (sorted)
   144→func (r *Registry) ListIDs() []string {
   145→	r.ensureInitialized()
   146→	r.mu.RLock()
   147→	defer r.mu.RUnlock()
   148→
   149→	ids := make([]string, 0, len(r.providers))
   150→	for id := range r.providers {
   151→		ids = append(ids, id)
   152→	}
   153→
   154→	sort.Strings(ids)
   155→	return ids
   156→}
   157→
   158→// GetDefault returns the default provider (Claude)
   159→func (r *Registry) GetDefault() (spi.Provider, error) {
   160→	r.ensureInitialized()
   161→	slog.Debug("Getting default provider (claude)")
   162→	return r.Get("claude")
   163→}
   164→
   165→// GetProviderList returns a formatted string listing all providers.
   166→// Used for help text and error messages.
   167→// The result is cached since providers are static for the lifetime of the process.
   168→func (r *Registry) GetProviderList() string {
   169→	r.ensureInitialized()
   170→
   171→	// Build the provider list only once and cache it
   172→	r.providerListOnce.Do(func() {
   173→		r.mu.RLock()
   174→		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
   175→
   176→		if len(r.providers) == 0 {
   177→			r.providerListCache = "No providers registered"
   178→			return
   179→		}
   180→
   181→		// Get sorted IDs for consistent ordering
   182→		ids := make([]string, 0, len(r.providers))
   183→		for id := range r.providers {
   184→			ids = append(ids, id)
   185→		}
   186→		sort.Strings(ids)
   187→
   188→		// Build the formatted list by accessing providers directly (no Get() calls)
   189→		var parts []string
   190→		for _, id := range ids {
   191→			if provider, ok := r.providers[id]; ok {
   192→				parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", id, provider.Name()))
   193→			}
   194→		}
   195→
   196→		r.providerListCache = strings.Join(parts, ", ")
   197→	})
   198→
   199→	return r.providerListCache
   200→}
   201→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
package markdown

import (
	"fmt"
	"sort"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
)

// Type aliases for convenience - use the shared schema types
type (
	SessionData  = schema.SessionData
	ProviderInfo = schema.ProviderInfo
	Exchange     = schema.Exchange
	Message      = schema.Message
	ContentPart  = schema.ContentPart
	ToolInfo     = schema.ToolInfo
)

// Markdown constants
const (
	GeneratedBySpecStory = "<!-- Generated by SpecStory, Markdown v2.1.0 -->"
	ToolUseOpenTag       = "<tool-use data-tool-type=\"%s\" data-tool-name=\"%s\"><details>"
	ToolUseCloseTag      = "</details></tool-use>"
)

// GenerateMarkdownFromAgentSession generates markdown from a SessionData
// This is provider-agnostic and works from the unified agent session JSON format
// If includeMessageIDs is true, message IDs will be included in the output
// useUTC controls timestamp format (true=UTC, false=local)
func GenerateMarkdownFromAgentSession(sessionData *SessionData, includeMessageIDs bool, useUTC bool) (string, error) {
	if sessionData == nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("sessionData is nil")
	}

	var markdown strings.Builder

	// Add header comment
	markdown.WriteString(GeneratedBySpecStory + "\n\n")

	// Create title from session info
	title := generateTitle(sessionData, useUTC)
	markdown.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("# %s\n\n", title))

	// Add session metadata comment using the same timestamp format as the title
	var commentTimestamp string
	if len(sessionData.Exchanges) > 0 && sessionData.Exchanges[0].StartTime != "" {
		commentTimestamp = formatTimestamp(sessionData.Exchanges[0].StartTime, useUTC)
```

 `./pkg/providers/claudecode/provider.go`

```text
     1→package claudecode
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"bufio"
     5→	"bytes"
     6→	"context"
     7→	"encoding/json"
     8→	"errors"
     9→	"fmt"
    10→	"io"
    11→	"log/slog"
    12→	"os"
    13→	"os/exec"
    14→	"path/filepath"
    15→	"strings"
    16→
    17→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/analytics"
    18→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/log"
    19→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
    20→)
    21→
    22→// Provider implements the SPI Provider interface for Claude Code
    23→type Provider struct{}
    24→
    25→// NewProvider creates a new Claude Code provider instance
    26→func NewProvider() *Provider {
    27→	return &Provider{}
    28→}
    29→
    30→// filterWarmupMessages removes warmup messages (sidechain messages before first real message)
    31→func filterWarmupMessages(records []JSONLRecord) []JSONLRecord {
    32→	// Find first non-sidechain message
    33→	firstRealMessageIndex := -1
    34→	for i, record := range records {
    35→		if isSidechain, ok := record.Data["isSidechain"].(bool); !ok || !isSidechain {
    36→			firstRealMessageIndex = i
    37→			break
    38→		}
    39→	}
    40→
    41→	// No real messages found
    42→	if firstRealMessageIndex == -1 {
    43→		return []JSONLRecord{}
    44→	}
    45→
    46→	// Return records starting from first real message
    47→	return records[firstRealMessageIndex:]
    48→}
    49→
    50→// processSession filters warmup messages and converts a Session to an AgentChatSession
    51→// Returns nil if the session is warmup-only (no real messages)
    52→func processSession(session Session, workspaceRoot string, debugRaw bool) *spi.AgentChatSession {
    53→	// Write debug files first (even for warmup-only sessions)
    54→	if debugRaw {
    55→		// Write debug files and get the record-to-file mapping
    56→		_ = writeDebugRawFiles(session) // Unused but needed for side effect
    57→	}
    58→
    59→	// Filter warmup messages
    60→	filteredRecords := filterWarmupMessages(session.Records)
    61→
    62→	// Skip if no real messages remain after filtering warmup
    63→	if len(filteredRecords) == 0 {
    64→		slog.Debug("Skipping warmup-only session", "sessionId", session.SessionUuid)
    65→		return nil
    66→	}
    67→
    68→	// Create session with filtered records
    69→	filteredSession := Session{
    70→		SessionUuid: session.SessionUuid,
    71→		Records:     filteredRecords,
    72→	}
    73→
    74→	// Get timestamp from root record
    75→	rootRecord := filteredSession.Records[0]
    76→	timestamp := rootRecord.Data["timestamp"].(string)
    77→
    78→	// Get the slug
    79→	slug := FileSlugFromRootRecord(filteredSession)
    80→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→package utils

     3→import (
     4→	"fmt"
     5→	"log/slog"
     6→	"os"
     7→	"path/filepath"
     8→)
     9→
    10→// Directory constants
    11→const SPECSTORY_DIR = ".specstory"
    12→const HISTORY_DIR = "history"
    13→const DEBUG_DIR = "debug"
    14→const DEBUG_LOG_FILE = "debug.log"
    15→
    16→// OutputConfig interface defines methods for getting output directories
    17→type OutputConfig interface {
    18→	GetHistoryDir() string
    19→	GetDebugDir() string
    20→}
    21→
    22→// OutputPathConfig manages all output directory configuration
    23→type OutputPathConfig struct {
    24→	BaseDir string // The validated absolute path
    25→}
    26→
    27→// Ensure OutputPathConfig implements OutputConfig interface
    28→var _ OutputConfig = (*OutputPathConfig)(nil)
    29→
    30→// NewOutputPathConfig creates and validates an output configuration
    31→func NewOutputPathConfig(dir string) (*OutputPathConfig, error) {
    32→	if dir == "" {
    33→		return &OutputPathConfig{}, nil // Use defaults
    34→	}
    35→
    36→	// Convert to absolute path if relative
    37→	absPath, err := filepath.Abs(dir)
    38→	if err != nil {
    39→		return nil, ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf("invalid output directory path: %v", err)}
    40→	}
    41→
    42→	// Check if path exists
    43→	info, err := os.Stat(absPath)
    44→	if err == nil {
    45→		// Path exists, check if it's a directory
    46→		if !info.IsDir() {
    47→			return nil, ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf("output path exists but is not a directory: %s", absPath)}
    48→		}
    49→		// Check write permissions by attempting to create a temp file
    50→		if file, err := os.CreateTemp(absPath, ".specstory_write_test_*"); err != nil {
    51→			return nil, ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf("output directory is not writable: %s", absPath)}
    52→		} else {
    53→			// Clean up test file
    54→			_ = file.Close()
    55→			_ = os.Remove(file.Name())
    56→		}
    57→		slog.Debug("Using existing output directory", "path", absPath)
    58→	} else if os.IsNotExist(err) {
    59→		// Path doesn't exist, try to create it
    60→		slog.Info("Creating output directory", "path", absPath)
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/intent/pkg/explorer/provenance.go`

```text
     1→package explorer
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"encoding/json"
     5→	"fmt"
     6→	"sort"
     7→
     8→	"github.com/specstoryai/intent/pkg/client"
     9→)
    10→
    11→// ProvenanceMode determines how provenance marks are displayed
    12→type ProvenanceMode int
    13→
    14→const (
    15→	ProvenanceOff    ProvenanceMode = iota
    16→	ProvenanceGutter                // Show bracket markers in gutter
    17→	ProvenanceInline                // Show popover on selected line
    18→)
    19→
    20→// ProvenanceInfo contains parsed provenance metadata
    21→type ProvenanceInfo struct {
    22→	AgentType     string
    23→	AgentModel    string
    24→	SessionID     string
    25→	ExchangeID    string
    26→	ActorHost     string
    27→	ActorUsername string
    28→	Timestamp     string
    29→}
    30→
    31→// LineMark represents a provenance mark mapped to line numbers
    32→type LineMark struct {
    33→	StartLine int
    34→	EndLine   int
    35→	Info      ProvenanceInfo
    36→	Summary   string
    37→}
    38→
    39→// BuildLineMarks converts TextMarks to line-based marks
    40→func BuildLineMarks(content string, marks []client.TextMark) []LineMark {
    41→	if len(marks) == 0 || content == "" {
    42→		return nil
    43→	}
    44→
    45→	// Build character index to line number mapping
    46→	runes := []rune(content)
    47→	lineForIndex := make([]int, len(runes)+1)
    48→	line := 1
    49→	for i := 0; i <= len(runes); i++ {
    50→		lineForIndex[i] = line
    51→		if i < len(runes) && runes[i] == '\n' {
    52→			line++
    53→		}
    54→	}
    55→
    56→	var result []LineMark
    57→	for _, mark := range marks {
    58→		if mark.Name != "intent:provenance" {
    59→			continue
    60→		}
    61→
    62→		info, ok := parseProvenanceValue(mark.Value)
    63→		if !ok {
    64→			continue
    65→		}
    66→
    67→		startLine := 1
    68→		endLine := 1
    69→		if mark.Start >= 0 && mark.Start < len(lineForIndex) {
    70→			startLine = lineForIndex[mark.Start]
    71→		}
    72→		if mark.End > 0 && mark.End <= len(lineForIndex) {
    73→			endLine = lineForIndex[mark.End-1] // End is exclusive
    74→		}
    75→		if endLine < startLine {
    76→			endLine = startLine
    77→		}
    78→
    79→		result = append(result, LineMark{
    80→			StartLine: startLine,
    81→			EndLine:   endLine,
    82→			Info:      info,
    83→			Summary:   formatProvenanceSummary(info),
    84→		})
    85→	}
    86→
    87→	// Sort by start line
    88→	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
    89→		return result[i].StartLine < result[j].StartLine
    90→	})
    91→
    92→	return result
    93→}
    94→
    95→// parseProvenanceValue extracts ProvenanceInfo from mark value
    96→func parseProvenanceValue(raw map[string]interface{}) (ProvenanceInfo, bool) {
    97→	if raw == nil {
    98→		return ProvenanceInfo{}, false
    99→	}
   100→
   101→	// Handle nested "intent:provenance" key if present
   102→	if nested, ok := raw["intent:provenance"]; ok {
   103→		switch v := nested.(type) {
   104→		case map[string]interface{}:
   105→			raw = v
   106→		case string:
   107→			var parsed map[string]interface{}
   108→			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(v), &parsed); err == nil {
   109→				raw = parsed
   110→			}
   111→		}
   112→	}
   113→
   114→	info := ProvenanceInfo{
   115→		AgentType:     stringFromAny(raw["agentType"]),
   116→		AgentModel:    stringFromAny(raw["agentModel"]),
   117→		SessionID:     stringFromAny(raw["sessionId"]),
   118→		ExchangeID:    stringFromAny(raw["exchangeId"]),
   119→		ActorHost:     stringFromAny(raw["actorHost"]),
   120→		ActorUsername: stringFromAny(raw["actorUsername"]),
   121→		Timestamp:     stringFromAny(raw["timestamp"]),
   122→	}
   123→
   124→	// Return false if no useful info
   125→	if info.AgentType == "" && info.AgentModel == "" &&
   126→		info.ActorHost == "" && info.ActorUsername == "" &&
   127→		info.SessionID == "" && info.ExchangeID == "" {
   128→		return ProvenanceInfo{}, false
   129→	}
   130→
   131→	return info, true
   132→}
   133→
   134→// stringFromAny safely converts interface{} to string
   135→func stringFromAny(val interface{}) string {
   136→	if s, ok := val.(string); ok {
   137→		return s
   138→	}
   139→	return ""
   140→}
   141→
   142→// formatProvenanceSummary formats a short summary of provenance
   143→func formatProvenanceSummary(info ProvenanceInfo) string {
   144→	agent := info.AgentType
   145→	if agent == "" {
   146→		agent = "unknown"
   147→	}
   148→	if info.AgentModel != "" {
   149→		agent = fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s", agent, info.AgentModel)
   150→	}
   151→
   152→	id := ""
   153→	if info.ExchangeID != "" {
   154→		short := info.ExchangeID
   155→		if len(short) > 8 {
   156→			short = short[:8]
   157→		}
   158→		id = fmt.Sprintf(" ex_%s", short)
   159→	} else if info.SessionID != "" {
   160→		short := info.SessionID
   161→		if len(short) > 8 {
   162→			short = short[:8]
   163→		}
   164→		id = fmt.Sprintf(" sess_%s", short)
   165→	}
   166→
   167→	return agent + id
   168→}
   169→
   170→// GroupMarksByLine groups marks by their start line for quick lookup
   171→func GroupMarksByLine(marks []LineMark) map[int][]LineMark {
   172→	result := make(map[int][]LineMark)
   173→	for _, m := range marks {
   174→		result[m.StartLine] = append(result[m.StartLine], m)
   175→	}
   176→	return result
   177→}
   178→
```

```text
     1→package tui

     7→
     8→type provenanceInfo struct {

    10→	AgentModel    string
    11→	SessionID     string
    12→	ExchangeID    string
    13→	ActorHost     string
    14→	ActorUsername string
    15→	Timestamp     string
    16→}
    17→
    18→func provenanceFromMarksMap(marks map[string]interface{}) (provenanceInfo, bool) {
    19→	if marks == nil {
    20→		return provenanceInfo{}, false
    21→	}
    22→	raw, ok := marks["intent:provenance"]
    23→	if !ok || raw == nil {
    24→		return provenanceInfo{}, false
    25→	}
    26→	return provenanceFromValue(raw)
    27→}
    28→
    29→func provenanceFromValue(raw interface{}) (provenanceInfo, bool) {
    30→	data, ok := parseProvenanceValue(raw)
    31→	if !ok {
    32→		return provenanceInfo{}, false
    33→	}
    34→
    35→	info := provenanceInfo{
    36→		AgentType:     stringFromAny(data["agentType"]),
    37→		AgentModel:    stringFromAny(data["agentModel"]),
    38→		SessionID:     stringFromAny(data["sessionId"]),
    39→		ExchangeID:    stringFromAny(data["exchangeId"]),
    40→		ActorHost:     stringFromAny(data["actorHost"]),
    41→		ActorUsername: stringFromAny(data["actorUsername"]),
    42→		Timestamp:     stringFromAny(data["timestamp"]),
    43→	}
    44→
    45→	if info.AgentType == "" && info.AgentModel == "" && info.ActorHost == "" && info.ActorUsername == "" && info.SessionID == "" && info.ExchangeID == "" {
    46→		return provenanceInfo{}, false
    47→	}
    48→	return info, true
    49→}
    50→
    51→func parseProvenanceValue(raw interface{}) (map[string]interface{}, bool) {
    52→	switch v := raw.(type) {
    53→	case map[string]interface{}:
    54→		return v, true
    55→	case string:
    56→		var data map[string]interface{}
    57→		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(v), &data); err != nil {
    58→			return nil, false
    59→		}
    60→		return data, true
    61→	default:
    62→		return nil, false
    63→	}
    64→}
    65→
    66→func stringFromAny(val interface{}) string {
    67→	switch v := val.(type) {
    68→	case string:
    69→		return v
    70→	default:
    71→		return ""
    72→	}
    73→}
    74→
    75→func formatProvenanceSummary(info provenanceInfo) string {
    76→	agent := info.AgentType
    77→	if agent == "" {
    78→		agent = "unknown agent"
    79→	}
    80→	if info.AgentModel != "" {
    81→		agent = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", agent, info.AgentModel)
    82→	}
    83→
    84→	host := ""
    85→	if info.ActorUsername != "" && info.ActorHost != "" {
    86→		host = fmt.Sprintf(" @ %s@%s", info.ActorUsername, info.ActorHost)
    87→	} else if info.ActorUsername != "" {
    88→		host = fmt.Sprintf(" @ %s", info.ActorUsername)
    89→	} else if info.ActorHost != "" {
    90→		host = fmt.Sprintf(" @ %s", info.ActorHost)
    91→	}
    92→
    93→	id := ""
    94→	if info.ExchangeID != "" {
    95→		id = fmt.Sprintf(" • exchange %s", info.ExchangeID)
    96→	} else if info.SessionID != "" {
    97→		id = fmt.Sprintf(" • session %s", info.SessionID)
    98→	}
    99→
   100→	return fmt.Sprintf("Authored by %s%s%s", agent, host, id)
   101→}
   102→
   103→func formatTimelineProvenance(info provenanceInfo) string {
   104→	if info.AgentType == "" && info.AgentModel == "" && info.ActorHost == "" && info.ActorUsername == "" {
   105→		return ""
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	agent := info.AgentType
   109→	if agent == "" {
   110→		agent = "unknown"
   111→	}
   112→	if info.AgentModel != "" {
   113→		agent = fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s", agent, info.AgentModel)
   114→	}
   115→
   116→	host := ""
   117→	if info.ActorUsername != "" && info.ActorHost != "" {
   118→		host = fmt.Sprintf("%s@%s", info.ActorUsername, info.ActorHost)
   119→	} else if info.ActorUsername != "" {
   120→		host = info.ActorUsername
   121→	} else if info.ActorHost != "" {
   122→		host = info.ActorHost
   123→	}
   124→
   125→	label := agent
   126→	if host != "" {
   127→		label = fmt.Sprintf("%s • %s", label, host)
   128→	}
   129→
   130→	return fmt.Sprintf(" [%s]", label)
   131→}
   132→
```

```text
     1→// Package agentobserver provides the bridge between SpecStory CLI's agent observer
     2→// and Intent's journal system. It extracts file operations from agent sessions.
     3→package agentobserver
     4→
     5→import (
     6→	"context"
     7→	"encoding/json"
     8→	"fmt"
     9→	"log/slog"
    10→	"path/filepath"
    11→	"strings"
    12→	"sync"
    13→	"time"
    14→
    15→	"github.com/google/uuid"
    16→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/markdown"
    17→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
    18→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
    19→	"github.com/specstoryai/intent/pkg/actor"
    20→	"github.com/specstoryai/intent/pkg/journal"
    21→	"github.com/specstoryai/intent/pkg/outbox"
    22→	"github.com/specstoryai/intent/pkg/pathutil"
    23→)
    24→
    25→// Confidence levels for file attribution
    26→const (
    27→	ConfidenceDeclared = "declared" // Path explicitly provided by tool input or pathHints
    28→	ConfidenceInferred = "inferred" // Path derived from other sources
    29→)
    30→
    31→// Observer handles agent session updates and writes provenance hints to the journal.
    32→// It bridges the SpecStory CLI agent observer with Intent's journal system.
    33→type Observer struct {
    34→	journal       journal.Journal
    35→	outboxWriter  *journal.OutboxWriter
    36→	actorResolver *actor.Resolver
    37→	crdtManager   crdtManager // For appending session markdown to CRDTs
    38→	projectRoot   string      // Intent's canonical project root for path normalization
    39→	logger        *slog.Logger
    40→
    41→	// outboxStore enables real-time SQLite synchronization via events
    42→	outboxMu    sync.RWMutex
    43→	outboxStore *outbox.Store
    44→}
    45→
    46→// crdtManager defines the interface needed by Observer for CRDT operations.
    47→// This avoids a direct dependency on the crdt package.
    48→type crdtManager interface {
    49→	GetActiveEpisode() string
    50→	AppendAgentSessionMarkdown(ctx context.Context, episodeName, sessionID, markdown string) error
    51→}
    52→
    53→// NewObserver creates a new agent Observer with the given dependencies.
    54→// The projectRoot should be Intent's canonical project root, which is used
    55→// to normalize file paths consistently across different path representations
    56→// (e.g., on WSL where paths can have multiple representations).
    57→func NewObserver(j journal.Journal, outboxWriter *journal.OutboxWriter, actorResolver *actor.Resolver, cm crdtManager, projectRoot string) *Observer {
    58→	// Ensure we have the canonical project root for consistent path handling
    59→	canonicalRoot, err := pathutil.CanonicalProjectRoot(projectRoot)
    60→	if err != nil {
    61→		agentObserverLogger().Warn("Failed to canonicalize project root",
    62→			"projectRoot", projectRoot,
    63→			"error", err)
    64→		canonicalRoot = projectRoot
    65→	}
    66→
    67→	return &Observer{
    68→		journal:       j,
    69→		outboxWriter:  outboxWriter,
    70→		actorResolver: actorResolver,
    71→		crdtManager:   cm,
    72→		projectRoot:   canonicalRoot,
    73→		logger:        agentObserverLogger(),
    74→	}
    75→}
    76→
    77→// SetOutboxStore configures the outbox store for real-time SQLite synchronization.
    78→// When set, session updates will emit events that trigger SQLite upserts.
    79→func (o *Observer) SetOutboxStore(store *outbox.Store) {
    80→	o.outboxMu.Lock()
    81→	defer o.outboxMu.Unlock()
    82→	o.outboxStore = store
    83→}
    84→
    85→// HandleSessionUpdate is the callback for agent observer updates.
    86→// It extracts file operations and writes journal entries with Source=SourceAgent.
    87→func (o *Observer) HandleSessionUpdate(providerID string, session *spi.AgentChatSession) {
    88→	if session == nil || session.SessionData == nil {
    89→		return
    90→	}
    91→
    92→	sessionID := session.SessionID
    93→	agentWorkspaceRoot := session.SessionData.WorkspaceRoot
    94→
    95→	// Use Intent's canonical project root for path normalization.
    96→	// This ensures paths match those computed by the file watcher, even when
    97→	// the agent reports a different path representation (e.g., on WSL).
    98→	projectRoot := o.projectRoot
    99→	if projectRoot == "" {
   100→		// Fallback to agent's workspace root if Intent's root not set
   101→		projectRoot = agentWorkspaceRoot
   102→	}
   103→
   104→	// Log if the agent's workspace root differs from Intent's project root
   105→	// This helps diagnose path matching issues on WSL or case-insensitive filesystems
   106→	if agentWorkspaceRoot != "" && projectRoot != "" && !pathutil.PathsEqual(agentWorkspaceRoot, projectRoot) {
   107→		o.logger.Debug("Agent workspace root differs from Intent project root",
   108→			"agentWorkspaceRoot", agentWorkspaceRoot,
   109→			"intentProjectRoot", projectRoot,
   110→			"sessionID", sessionID,
   111→		)
   112→	}
   113→
   114→	o.logger.Debug("Session update",
   115→		"provider", providerID,
   116→		"sessionID", sessionID,
   117→		"exchanges", len(session.SessionData.Exchanges),
   118→		"projectRoot", projectRoot,
   119→	)
   120→
   121→	for exchangeIdx, exchange := range session.SessionData.Exchanges {
   122→		ops := ExtractFileOperations(&exchange, projectRoot)
   123→		if len(ops) == 0 {
   124→			continue
   125→		}
   126→
   127→		exchangeID := exchange.ExchangeID
   128→		if exchangeID == "" {
   129→			exchangeID = fmt.Sprintf("ex_%d_%s", exchangeIdx, sessionID)
   130→		}
   131→
   132→		// Extract agent info from the exchange
   133→		agentType := extractAgentType(providerID, &exchange)
   134→		agentModel := extractAgentModel(&exchange)
   135→		userPrompt := extractUserPrompt(&exchange)
   136→		agentReply := extractAgentReply(&exchange)
   137→
   138→		for idx, fileOp := range ops {
   139→			entry := o.buildProvenanceEntry(
   140→				sessionID,
   141→				exchangeID,
   142→				agentType,
   143→				agentModel,
   144→				userPrompt,
   145→				agentReply,
   146→				&exchange,
   147→				fileOp,
   148→				idx,
   149→				len(ops),
   150→			)
```

```text
     1→// Package provenance correlates filesystem changes with AI agent activity
     2→// to determine which file changes were caused by which agent interactions.
     3→package provenance
     4→
     5→import (
     6→	"errors"
     7→	"strings"
     8→	"time"
     9→)
    10→
    11→// Validation errors returned by the Validate methods.
    12→var (
    13→	ErrMissingID         = errors.New("ID is required")
    14→	ErrMissingPath       = errors.New("path is required")
    15→	ErrPathNotAbsolute   = errors.New("path must be absolute (start with /)")
    16→	ErrMissingTimestamp  = errors.New("timestamp is required")
    17→	ErrMissingChangeType = errors.New("change type is required")
    18→	ErrMissingSessionID  = errors.New("session ID is required")
    19→	ErrMissingExchangeID = errors.New("exchange ID is required")
    20→	ErrMissingAgentType  = errors.New("agent type is required")
    21→)
    22→
    23→// FileEvent represents a filesystem change detected by the consumer's file watcher.
    24→// Paths must be absolute with forward-slash delimiters (e.g. /Users/sean/project/src/foo.go).
    25→type FileEvent struct {
    26→	ID         string    // Unique identifier
    27→	Path       string    // Absolute path, forward slashes
    28→	ChangeType string    // "create", "modify", "delete", "rename"
    29→	Timestamp  time.Time // When the file changed (file ModTime)
    30→}
    31→
    32→// Validate checks that a FileEvent has all required fields and that the path is absolute.
    33→func (e FileEvent) Validate() error {
    34→	if e.ID == "" {
    35→		return ErrMissingID
    36→	}
    37→	if e.Path == "" {
    38→		return ErrMissingPath
    39→	}
    40→	if !strings.HasPrefix(e.Path, "/") {
    41→		return ErrPathNotAbsolute
    42→	}
    43→	if e.ChangeType == "" {
    44→		return ErrMissingChangeType
    45→	}
    46→	if e.Timestamp.IsZero() {
    47→		return ErrMissingTimestamp
    48→	}
    49→	return nil
    50→}
    51→
    52→// AgentEvent represents a file operation reported by an AI agent session.
    53→// Paths may be relative or absolute — the library normalizes them before storage.
    54→type AgentEvent struct {
    55→	ID            string    // Deterministic ID for deduplication
    56→	FilePath      string    // Path the agent touched (may be relative)
    57→	ChangeType    string    // "create", "edit", "write", "delete"
    58→	Timestamp     time.Time // When the operation was recorded
    59→	SessionID     string    // Agent session ID
    60→	ExchangeID    string    // Specific exchange within session
    61→	MessageID     string    // Agent message ID
    62→	AgentType     string    // "claude-code", "cursor", etc.
    63→	AgentModel    string    // "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", etc.
    64→	ActorHost     string    // Machine hostname
    65→	ActorUsername string    // OS user
    66→}
    67→
    68→// Validate checks that an AgentEvent has all required fields.
    69→func (e AgentEvent) Validate() error {
    70→	if e.ID == "" {
    71→		return ErrMissingID
    72→	}
    73→	if e.FilePath == "" {
    74→		return ErrMissingPath
    75→	}
    76→	if e.ChangeType == "" {
    77→		return ErrMissingChangeType
    78→	}
    79→	if e.Timestamp.IsZero() {
    80→		return ErrMissingTimestamp
    81→	}
    82→	if e.SessionID == "" {
    83→		return ErrMissingSessionID
    84→	}
    85→	if e.ExchangeID == "" {
    86→		return ErrMissingExchangeID
    87→	}
    88→	if e.AgentType == "" {
    89→		return ErrMissingAgentType
    90→	}
    91→	return nil
    92→}
    93→
    94→// NormalizePath normalizes an agent file path for storage and matching.
    95→// Backslashes are replaced with forward slashes, and a leading "/" is added if missing.
    96→func NormalizePath(path string) string {
    97→	path = strings.ReplaceAll(path, "\\", "/")
    98→	if !strings.HasPrefix(path, "/") {
    99→		path = "/" + path
   100→	}
   101→	return path
   102→}
   103→
   104→// ProvenanceRecord is the output of correlation — a file change attributed to an agent exchange.
   105→type ProvenanceRecord struct {
   106→	// File data
   107→	FilePath string // Absolute path, forward slashes
   108→	ChangeType  string    // "create", "modify", "delete", "rename"
   109→	Timestamp   time.Time // When file changed
   110→
   111→	// Attribution
   112→	SessionID  string // Agent session that caused the change
   113→	ExchangeID string // Specific exchange that caused the change
   114→	AgentType  string // "claude-code", "cursor", etc.
   115→	AgentModel string // Model used
   116→	MessageID  string // For tracing
   117→
   118→	// Actor info
   119→	ActorHost     string
   120→	ActorUsername string
   121→
   122→	// Correlation metadata
   123→	MatchedAt time.Time // When correlation occurred
   124→}
   125→
```

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/ai-provenance-lib/engine.go`

```text
     1→package provenance
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"os"
     8→	"path/filepath"
     9→	"strings"
    10→	"time"
    11→)
    12→
    13→const defaultMatchWindow = 5 * time.Second
    14→
    15→// defaultDBPath returns ~/.specstory/provenance/provenance.db.
    16→// Returns an error if the home directory cannot be determined.
    17→func defaultDBPath() (string, error) {
    18→	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
    19→	if err != nil {
    20→		return "", fmt.Errorf("determining home directory: %w", err)
    21→	}
    22→	return filepath.Join(home, ".specstory", "provenance", "provenance.db"), nil
    23→}
    24→
    25→// Option configures the Engine.
    26→type Option func(*Engine)
    27→
    28→// WithDBPath overrides the default database location (~/.specstory/provenance/provenance.db).
    29→func WithDBPath(path string) Option {
    30→	return func(e *Engine) { e.dbPath = path }
    31→}
    32→
    33→// WithMatchWindow sets the time window for correlating file events to agent events.
    34→// A file event and agent event match only if their timestamps are within ±window.
    35→// Defaults to 5 seconds.
    36→func WithMatchWindow(d time.Duration) Option {
    37→	return func(e *Engine) { e.matchWindow = d }
    38→}
    39→
    40→// Engine correlates file events to agent activity and produces provenance records.
    41→type Engine struct {
    42→	dbPath      string
    43→	store       *Store
    44→	matchWindow time.Duration
    45→}
    46→
    47→// NewEngine creates a correlation engine backed by a SQLite database.
    48→// By default the database is stored at ~/.specstory/provenance/provenance.db.
    49→// Use WithDBPath to override.
    50→func NewEngine(opts ...Option) (*Engine, error) {
    51→	e := &Engine{
    52→		matchWindow: defaultMatchWindow,
    53→	}
    54→	for _, opt := range opts {
    55→		opt(e)
    56→	}
    57→
    58→	// Resolve the default DB path only if no override was provided.
    59→	if e.dbPath == "" {
    60→		path, err := defaultDBPath()
    61→		if err != nil {
    62→			return nil, err
    63→		}
    64→		e.dbPath = path
    65→	}
    66→
    67→	store, err := OpenStore(e.dbPath)
    68→	if err != nil {
    69→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("opening store: %w", err)
    70→	}
    71→	e.store = store
    72→
    73→	provenanceLogger().Info("Provenance engine started",
    74→		"dbPath", e.dbPath,
    75→		"matchWindow", e.matchWindow)
    76→
    77→	return e, nil
    78→}
    79→
    80→// Close closes the underlying database connection.
    81→func (e *Engine) Close() error {
    82→	return e.store.Close()
    83→}
    84→
    85→// PushFileEvent stores a file event and attempts to correlate it with an existing
    86→// unmatched agent event. Returns a ProvenanceRecord if a match is found, nil otherwise.
    87→func (e *Engine) PushFileEvent(ctx context.Context, event FileEvent) (*ProvenanceRecord, error) {
    88→	if err := e.store.PushFileEvent(ctx, event); err != nil {
    89→		return nil, err
    90→	}
    91→
    92→	// Only fetch agent events within the match window — events outside
    93→	// this range can never correlate, so there's no reason to load them.
    94→	since := event.Timestamp.Add(-e.matchWindow)
    95→	until := event.Timestamp.Add(e.matchWindow)
    96→	agentEvents, err := e.store.QueryUnmatchedAgentEvents(ctx, since, until)
    97→	if err != nil {
    98→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("querying unmatched agent events: %w", err)
    99→	}
   100→
   101→	best := findBestMatch(event.Timestamp, agentEvents, e.matchWindow, func(agentPath string) bool {
   102→		return pathSuffixMatch(event.Path, agentPath)
   103→	})
   104→	if best == nil {
   105→		provenanceLogger().Debug("No agent match for file event",
   106→			"fileEventID", event.ID,
   107→			"filePath", event.Path,
   108→			"candidates", len(agentEvents))
   109→		return nil, nil
   110→	}
   111→
   112→	var agentEvt AgentEvent
   113→	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(best.Payload), &agentEvt); err != nil {
   114→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unmarshaling agent event payload: %w", err)
   115→	}
   116→
   117→	delta := absDuration(event.Timestamp.Sub(best.Timestamp))
   118→	record := buildRecord(event, agentEvt)
   119→
   120→	if err := e.store.SetMatchedWith(ctx, event.ID, best.ID); err != nil {
   121→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("setting matched: %w", err)
   122→	}
   123→
   124→	provenanceLogger().Info("Matched file event to agent event",
   125→		"fileEventID", event.ID,
   126→		"agentEventID", best.ID,
   127→		"filePath", event.Path,
   128→		"delta", delta)
   129→
   130→	return &record, nil
   131→}
   132→
   133→// PushAgentEvent stores an agent event and attempts to correlate it with an existing
   134→// unmatched file event. Returns a ProvenanceRecord if a match is found, nil otherwise.
   135→func (e *Engine) PushAgentEvent(ctx context.Context, event AgentEvent) (*ProvenanceRecord, error) {
   136→	if err := e.store.PushAgentEvent(ctx, event); err != nil {
   137→		return nil, err
   138→	}
   139→
   140→	normalizedPath := NormalizePath(event.FilePath)
   141→
   142→	// Only fetch file events within the match window — events outside
   143→	// this range can never correlate, so there's no reason to load them.
   144→	since := event.Timestamp.Add(-e.matchWindow)
   145→	until := event.Timestamp.Add(e.matchWindow)
   146→	fileEvents, err := e.store.QueryUnmatchedFileEvents(ctx, since, until)
   147→	if err != nil {
   148→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("querying unmatched file events: %w", err)
   149→	}
   150→
   151→	bestFE := findBestMatch(event.Timestamp, fileEvents, e.matchWindow, func(fePath string) bool {
   152→		return pathSuffixMatch(fePath, normalizedPath)
   153→	})
   154→	if bestFE == nil {
   155→		provenanceLogger().Debug("No file match for agent event",
   156→			"agentEventID", event.ID,
   157→			"agentPath", event.FilePath,
   158→			"candidates", len(fileEvents))
   159→		return nil, nil
   160→	}
   161→
   162→	// Reconstruct the FileEvent from the stored payload
   163→	var fileEvt FileEvent
   164→	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(bestFE.Payload), &fileEvt); err != nil {
   165→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unmarshaling file event payload: %w", err)
   166→	}
   167→
   168→	delta := absDuration(event.Timestamp.Sub(bestFE.Timestamp))
   169→	record := buildRecord(fileEvt, event)
   170→
   171→	if err := e.store.SetMatchedWith(ctx, bestFE.ID, event.ID); err != nil {
   172→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("setting matched: %w", err)
   173→	}
   174→
   175→	provenanceLogger().Info("Matched agent event to file event",
   176→		"agentEventID", event.ID,
   177→		"fileEventID", bestFE.ID,
   178→		"filePath", bestFE.FilePath,
   179→		"delta", delta)
   180→
   181→	return &record, nil
   182→}
   183→
   184→// findBestMatch finds the candidate closest in time whose path satisfies pathMatches.
   185→// Returns nil if no candidate matches within the window.
   186→func findBestMatch(refTime time.Time, candidates []unmatchedEvent, window time.Duration, pathMatches func(candidatePath string) bool) *unmatchedEvent {
   187→	var best *unmatchedEvent
   188→	var bestDelta time.Duration
   189→
   190→	for i := range candidates {
   191→		candidate := &candidates[i]
   192→		if !pathMatches(candidate.FilePath) {
   193→			continue
   194→		}
   195→		delta := absDuration(refTime.Sub(candidate.Timestamp))
   196→		if delta > window {
   197→			continue
   198→		}
   199→		if best == nil || delta < bestDelta {
   200→			best = candidate
   201→			bestDelta = delta
   202→		}
   203→	}
   204→
   205→	return best
   206→}
   207→
   208→// pathSuffixMatch returns true if the normalized agent path is a suffix of the FS path
   209→// aligned to a directory boundary. A raw suffix like "/foo.go" inside "/project/afoo.go"
   210→// is rejected because the match doesn't start at a "/" separator.
   211→func pathSuffixMatch(fsPath, agentPath string) bool {
   212→	if !strings.HasSuffix(fsPath, agentPath) {
   213→		return false
   214→	}
   215→	if len(fsPath) == len(agentPath) {
   216→		return true
   217→	}
   218→	// The character just before the matched suffix must be '/' to ensure
   219→	// we're matching at a directory boundary, not mid-filename.
   220→	return fsPath[len(fsPath)-len(agentPath)] == '/'
   221→}
   222→
   223→func absDuration(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
   224→	if d < 0 {
   225→		return -d
   226→	}
   227→	return d
   228→}
   229→
   230→// buildRecord creates a ProvenanceRecord from a matched file event + agent event pair.
   231→func buildRecord(fe FileEvent, ae AgentEvent) ProvenanceRecord {
   232→	return ProvenanceRecord{
   233→		FilePath: fe.Path,
   234→		ChangeType:    fe.ChangeType,
   235→		Timestamp:     fe.Timestamp,
   236→		SessionID:     ae.SessionID,
   237→		ExchangeID:    ae.ExchangeID,
   238→		AgentType:     ae.AgentType,
   239→		AgentModel:    ae.AgentModel,
   240→		MessageID:     ae.MessageID,
   241→		ActorHost:     ae.ActorHost,
   242→		ActorUsername: ae.ActorUsername,
   243→		MatchedAt: time.Now(),
   244→	}
   245→}
   246→
```

```text
     1→package provenance
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"database/sql"
     6→	"encoding/json"
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"os"
     9→	"path/filepath"
    10→	"time"
    11→
    12→	_ "modernc.org/sqlite" // SQLite driver (pure Go, same as Intent)
    13→)
    14→
    15→// Store handles SQLite persistence for file events and agent events.
    16→// It uses WAL mode for safe concurrent access from multiple processes.
    17→type Store struct {
    18→	db *sql.DB
    19→}
    20→
    21→// OpenStore opens (or creates) a SQLite database at the given path.
    22→// Applies WAL mode and performance pragmas matching Intent's configuration.
    23→func OpenStore(path string) (*Store, error) {
    24→	dir := filepath.Dir(path)
    25→	if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
    26→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating database directory: %w", err)
    27→	}
    28→
    29→	dsn := fmt.Sprintf("file:%s?_pragma=busy_timeout=15000&_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)", filepath.ToSlash(path))
    30→	db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dsn)
    31→	if err != nil {
    32→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("opening database: %w", err)
    33→	}
    34→
    35→	// Serialize writes to avoid deadlocks (same pattern as Intent)
    36→	db.SetMaxOpenConns(1)
    37→	db.SetMaxIdleConns(1)
    38→
    39→	// Performance pragmas matching Intent's sqlite.go
    40→	pragmas := []string{
    41→		"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL",
    42→		"PRAGMA cache_size = -64000",
    43→		"PRAGMA temp_store = MEMORY",
    44→		"PRAGMA mmap_size = 268435456",
    45→		"PRAGMA page_size = 8192",
    46→	}
    47→	for _, p := range pragmas {
    48→		if _, err := db.Exec(p); err != nil {
    49→			_ = db.Close()
    50→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("setting pragma %q: %w", p, err)
    51→		}
    52→	}
    53→
    54→	s := &Store{db: db}
    55→	if err := s.ensureSchema(); err != nil {
    56→		_ = db.Close()
    57→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("ensuring schema: %w", err)
    58→	}
    59→
    60→	return s, nil
    61→}
    62→
    63→// Close closes the database connection.
    64→func (s *Store) Close() error {
    65→	return s.db.Close()
    66→}
    67→
    68→func (s *Store) ensureSchema() error {
    69→	schema := `
    70→	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (
    71→		id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    72→		type TEXT NOT NULL,
    73→		file_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    74→		timestamp TEXT NOT NULL,
    75→		matched_with TEXT,
    76→		payload TEXT NOT NULL
    77→	);
    78→	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_unmatched
    79→		ON events(type, timestamp) WHERE matched_with IS NULL;
    80→	`
    81→	_, err := s.db.Exec(schema)
    82→	return err
    83→}
    84→
    85→// PushFileEvent stores a file event for later correlation.
    86→func (s *Store) PushFileEvent(ctx context.Context, event FileEvent) error {
    87→	if err := event.Validate(); err != nil {
    88→		return fmt.Errorf("invalid file event: %w", err)
    89→	}
    90→
    91→	payload, err := json.Marshal(event)
    92→	if err != nil {
    93→		return fmt.Errorf("marshaling file event: %w", err)
    94→	}
    95→
    96→	_, err = s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
    97→		`INSERT OR IGNORE INTO events (id, type, file_path, timestamp, payload) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
    98→		event.ID, "file_event", event.Path, event.Timestamp.Format(time.RFC3339Nano), string(payload),
    99→	)
   100→	if err != nil {
   101→		return fmt.Errorf("inserting file event: %w", err)
   102→	}
   103→
   104→	return nil
   105→}
   106→
   107→// PushAgentEvent stores an agent event for later correlation.
   108→// The file path is normalized before storage (backslashes → forward slashes, leading / added).
   109→func (s *Store) PushAgentEvent(ctx context.Context, event AgentEvent) error {
   110→	if err := event.Validate(); err != nil {
   111→		return fmt.Errorf("invalid agent event: %w", err)
   112→	}
   113→
   114→	normalizedPath := NormalizePath(event.FilePath)
   115→
   116→	payload, err := json.Marshal(event)
   117→	if err != nil {
   118→		return fmt.Errorf("marshaling agent event: %w", err)
   119→	}
   120→
   121→	_, err = s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
   122→		`INSERT OR IGNORE INTO events (id, type, file_path, timestamp, payload) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
   123→		event.ID, "agent_event", normalizedPath, event.Timestamp.Format(time.RFC3339Nano), string(payload),
   124→	)
   125→	if err != nil {
   126→		return fmt.Errorf("inserting agent event: %w", err)
   127→	}
   128→
   129→	return nil
   130→}
   131→
   132→// unmatchedEvent is an internal row representation used during correlation.
   133→type unmatchedEvent struct {
   134→	ID        string
   135→	Type      string
   136→	FilePath  string
   137→	Timestamp time.Time
   138→	Payload   string
   139→}
   140→
   141→// QueryUnmatchedFileEvents returns file events that haven't been correlated yet
   142→// and fall within the given time range, ordered by timestamp ascending.
   143→func (s *Store) QueryUnmatchedFileEvents(ctx context.Context, since, until time.Time) ([]unmatchedEvent, error) {
   144→	return s.queryUnmatched(ctx, "file_event", since, until)
   145→}
   146→
   147→// QueryUnmatchedAgentEvents returns agent events that haven't been correlated yet
   148→// and fall within the given time range, ordered by timestamp ascending.
   149→func (s *Store) QueryUnmatchedAgentEvents(ctx context.Context, since, until time.Time) ([]unmatchedEvent, error) {
   150→	return s.queryUnmatched(ctx, "agent_event", since, until)
   151→}
   152→
   153→func (s *Store) queryUnmatched(ctx context.Context, eventType string, since, until time.Time) ([]unmatchedEvent, error) {
   154→	rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
   155→		`SELECT id, type, file_path, timestamp, payload
   156→		 FROM events
   157→		 WHERE type = ? AND matched_with IS NULL
   158→		   AND timestamp >= ? AND timestamp <= ?
   159→		 ORDER BY timestamp`,
   160→		eventType, since.Format(time.RFC3339Nano), until.Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
   161→	)
   162→	if err != nil {
   163→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("querying unmatched %s: %w", eventType, err)
   164→	}
   165→	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
   166→
   167→	var events []unmatchedEvent
   168→	for rows.Next() {
   169→		var e unmatchedEvent
   170→		var ts string
   171→		if err := rows.Scan(&e.ID, &e.Type, &e.FilePath, &ts, &e.Payload); err != nil {
   172→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("scanning row: %w", err)
   173→		}
   174→		e.Timestamp, err = time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, ts)
   175→		if err != nil {
   176→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("parsing timestamp %q: %w", ts, err)
   177→		}
   178→		events = append(events, e)
   179→	}
   180→
   181→	return events, rows.Err()
   182→}
   183→
   184→// SetMatchedWith marks two events as correlated with each other in a single transaction.
   185→func (s *Store) SetMatchedWith(ctx context.Context, eventID, matchedWithID string) error {
   186→	tx, err := s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
   187→	if err != nil {
   188→		return fmt.Errorf("beginning transaction: %w", err)
   189→	}
   190→	defer func() { _ = tx.Rollback() }()
   191→
   192→	_, err = tx.ExecContext(ctx,
   193→		`UPDATE events SET matched_with = ? WHERE id = ?`,
   194→		matchedWithID, eventID,
   195→	)
   196→	if err != nil {
   197→		return fmt.Errorf("updating event %s: %w", eventID, err)
   198→	}
   199→
   200→	_, err = tx.ExecContext(ctx,
   201→		`UPDATE events SET matched_with = ? WHERE id = ?`,
   202→		eventID, matchedWithID,
   203→	)
   204→	if err != nil {
   205→		return fmt.Errorf("updating event %s: %w", matchedWithID, err)
   206→	}
   207→
   208→	return tx.Commit()
   209→}
   210→
```
