  1585→Specify a specific agent ID to check only a specific coding agent.`,
  1586→		Example: examples,
  1587→		Args:    cobra.MaximumNArgs(1), // Accept 0 or 1 argument
  1588→		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
  1589→			slog.Info("Running in check-install mode")
  1590→			registry := factory.GetRegistry()
  1591→
  1592→			// Get custom command if provided via flag
  1593→			customCmd, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("command")
  1594→
  1595→			// Validate that -c flag requires a provider
  1596→			if customCmd != "" && len(args) == 0 {
  1597→				registry := factory.GetRegistry()
  1598→				ids := registry.ListIDs()
  1599→				example := "specstory check <provider> -c \"/custom/path/to/agent\""
  1600→				if len(ids) > 0 {
  1601→					example = fmt.Sprintf("specstory check %s -c \"/custom/path/to/agent\"", ids[0])
  1602→				}
  1603→				return utils.ValidationError{
  1604→					Message: "The -c/--command flag requires a provider to be specified.\n" +
  1605→						"Example: " + example,
  1606→				}
  1607→			}
  1608→
  1609→			if len(args) == 0 {
  1610→				// Check all providers
  1611→				return checkAllProviders(registry)
  1612→			} else {
  1613→				// Check specific provider
  1614→				return checkSingleProvider(registry, args[0], customCmd)
  1615→			}
  1616→		},
  1617→	}
  1618→}
  1619→
  1620→var checkCmd *cobra.Command
  1621→
  1622→// printDivider prints a divider line for visual separation
  1623→func printDivider() {
  1624→	fmt.Println("\n--------")
  1625→}
  1626→
  1627→// checkSingleProvider checks a specific provider
  1628→func checkSingleProvider(registry *factory.Registry, providerID, customCmd string) error {
  1629→	provider, err := registry.Get(providerID)
  1630→	if err != nil {
  1631→		// Provider not found - show helpful error
  1632→		fmt.Printf("❌ Provider '%s' is not a valid provider implementation\n\n", providerID)
  1633→
  1634→		ids := registry.ListIDs()
  1635→		if len(ids) > 0 {
  1636→			fmt.Println("The registered providers are:")
  1637→			for _, id := range ids {
  1638→				if p, _ := registry.Get(id); p != nil {
  1639→					fmt.Printf("  • %s - %s\n", id, p.Name())
  1640→				}
  1641→			}
  1642→			fmt.Println("\nExample: specstory check " + ids[0])
  1643→		}
  1644→		return err
  1645→	}
  1646→
  1647→	// Set the agent provider for analytics
  1648→	analytics.SetAgentProviders([]string{provider.Name()})
  1649→
  1650→	// Run the check
  1651→	result := provider.Check(customCmd)
  1652→
  1653→	// Display results with the nice formatting
  1654→	if result.Success {
  1655→		fmt.Printf("\n✨ %s is installed and ready! ✨\n\n", provider.Name())
  1656→		fmt.Printf("  📦 Version: %s\n", result.Version)
  1657→		fmt.Printf("  📍 Location: %s\n", result.Location)
  1658→		fmt.Printf("  ✅ Status: All systems go!\n\n")
  1659→
  1660→		fmt.Println("🚀 Ready to sync your sessions! 💪")
  1661→		normalizedID := strings.ToLower(providerID)
  1662→		fmt.Printf("   • specstory run %s\n", normalizedID)
  1663→		fmt.Println("   • specstory sync - Save markdown files for existing sessions")
  1664→		fmt.Println()
  1665→
  1666→		return nil
  1667→	} else {
  1668→		fmt.Printf("\n❌ %s check failed!\n", provider.Name())
  1669→		if result.ErrorMessage != "" {
  1670→			fmt.Printf("\n%s\n", result.ErrorMessage)
  1671→		}
  1672→		return errors.New("check failed")
  1673→	}
  1674→}
  1675→
  1676→// checkAllProviders checks all registered providers
  1677→func checkAllProviders(registry *factory.Registry) error {
  1678→	// Sort for consistent output
  1679→	ids := registry.ListIDs()
  1680→
  1681→	// Collect all provider names for analytics
  1682→	var providerNames []string
  1683→	for _, id := range ids {
  1684→		if provider, err := registry.Get(id); err == nil {
  1685→			providerNames = append(providerNames, provider.Name())
  1686→		}
  1687→	}
  1688→	analytics.SetAgentProviders(providerNames)
  1689→
  1690→	anySuccess := false
  1691→	type providerInfo struct {
  1692→		id   string
  1693→		name string
  1694→	}
  1695→	var successfulProviders []providerInfo
  1696→	first := true
  1697→
  1698→	for _, id := range ids {
  1699→		provider, _ := registry.Get(id)
  1700→		// Invoke Check() here to keep registry limited to registration/lookup
  1701→		result := provider.Check("")
  1702→
  1703→		// Add divider between providers (but not before the first one)
  1704→		if !first {
  1705→			printDivider()
  1706→		}
  1707→		first = false
  1708→
  1709→		if result.Success {
  1710→			anySuccess = true
  1711→			successfulProviders = append(successfulProviders, providerInfo{id: id, name: provider.Name()})
  1712→			fmt.Printf("\n✨ %s is installed and ready! ✨\n\n", provider.Name())
  1713→			fmt.Printf("  📦 Version: %s\n", result.Version)
  1714→			fmt.Printf("  📍 Location: %s\n", result.Location)
  1715→			fmt.Printf("  ✅ Status: All systems go!\n")
  1716→		} else {
  1717→			fmt.Printf("\n❌ %s check failed!\n\n", provider.Name())
  1718→			if result.ErrorMessage != "" {
  1719→				// Show just first line of error for summary view
  1720→				lines := strings.Split(result.ErrorMessage, "\n")
  1721→				fmt.Printf("  Error: %s\n", strings.TrimSpace(lines[0]))
  1722→			}
  1723→		}
  1724→	}
  1725→
  1726→	// Show ready message if at least one provider is working
  1727→	if anySuccess {
  1728→		printDivider()
  1729→		fmt.Println("\n🚀 Ready to sync your sessions! 💪")
  1730→		for _, info := range successfulProviders {
  1731→			fmt.Printf("   • specstory run %s\n", info.id)
  1732→		}
  1733→		fmt.Println("   • specstory sync - Save markdown files for existing sessions")
  1734→		fmt.Println()
  1735→	} else {
  1736→		printDivider()
  1737→		fmt.Println("\n⚠️  No providers are currently available")
  1738→		fmt.Println("   Install at least one provider to use SpecStory")
  1739→		fmt.Println("\n💡 Tip: Use 'specstory check <provider>' for detailed installation help")
  1740→
  1741→		// Try to show an example with the first registered provider ID
  1742→		ids := registry.ListIDs()
  1743→		if len(ids) > 0 {
  1744→			fmt.Printf("   Example: specstory check %s\n", ids[0])
  1745→		} else {
  1746→			fmt.Println("   Example: specstory check <provider>")
  1747→		}
  1748→	}
  1749→
  1750→	// Return error only if ALL providers failed
  1751→	if !anySuccess {
  1752→		return errors.New("check failed")
  1753→	}
  1754→
  1755→	return nil
  1756→}
  1757→
  1758→// Command to log in to SpecStory Cloud
  1759→var loginCmd = &cobra.Command{
  1760→	Use:   "login",
  1761→	Short: "Log in to SpecStory Cloud",
  1762→	Long:  `Log in to SpecStory Cloud to enable cloud sync functionality.`,
  1763→	Example: `
  1764→# Log in
  1765→specstory login`,
  1766→	RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
  1767→		slog.Info("Running login command")
  1768→
  1769→		// Check if user is already authenticated
  1770→		if cloud.IsAuthenticated() {
  1771→			// Get user details
  1772→			username, loginTime := cloud.AuthenticatedAs()
  1773→
  1774→			// Already logged in - be friendly and show who they are!
  1775→			fmt.Println()
  1776→			fmt.Println("🔐 You're already logged in!")
  1777→			fmt.Println()
  1778→
  1779→			// Show user details if available
  1780→			if username != "" {
  1781→				fmt.Printf("👤 Logged in as: %s\n", username)
  1782→			}
  1783→			if loginTime != "" {
  1784→				// Parse and format the login time
  1785→				if t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, loginTime); err == nil {
  1786→					fmt.Printf("🕐 Logged in at: %s (UTC)\n", t.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05"))
  1787→				} else {
  1788→					// Fallback to raw time if parsing fails
  1789→					fmt.Printf("🕐 Logged in at: %s\n", loginTime)
  1790→				}
  1791→			}
  1792→
  1793→			fmt.Println()
  1794→			fmt.Println("🚀 Ready to sync with SpecStory Cloud! 💪")
  1795→			fmt.Println()
  1796→			return nil
  1797→		}
  1798→
  1799→		// User is not authenticated - start login flow
  1800→		slog.Info("Starting login flow")
  1801→		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventLoginAttempted, nil)
  1802→		loginURL := cloud.GetAPIBaseURL() + "/cli-login"
  1803→
  1804→		fmt.Println()
  1805→		fmt.Println("🌐 Opening your browser to log in to SpecStory Cloud...")
  1806→		fmt.Println()
  1807→
  1808→		// Try to open the browser
  1809→		if err := openBrowser(loginURL); err != nil {
  1810→			slog.Warn("Failed to open browser automatically", "error", err)
  1811→			// Don't fail, just inform the user
  1812→		}
  1813→
  1814→		// Display the URL for manual access
  1815→		fmt.Println("📋 If your browser didn't open, please visit:")
  1816→		fmt.Printf("   %s\n", loginURL)
  1817→		fmt.Println()
  1818→
  1819→		// Loop to allow retrying code entry
  1820→		reader := bufio.NewReader(os.Stdin)
  1821→
  1822→		// Track number of invalid attempts
  1823→		const maxAttempts = 5
  1824→		var invalidAttempts int
  1825→
  1826→		for {
  1827→			fmt.Println("🔑 Enter the 6-character code shown in your browser (or 'quit' to cancel):")
  1828→			fmt.Print("   Code: ")
  1829→
  1830→			// Read the code from user input
  1831→			code, err := reader.ReadString('\n')
  1832→			if err != nil {
  1833→				slog.Error("Failed to read authentication code", "error", err)
  1834→				analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventLoginFailed, analytics.Properties{
  1835→					"error": err.Error(),
  1836→					"stage": "reading_code",
  1837→				})
  1838→				return fmt.Errorf("failed to read authentication code: %w", err)
  1839→			}
  1840→
  1841→			// Trim whitespace
  1842→			code = strings.TrimSpace(code)
  1843→
  1844→			// Check if user wants to quit (case-insensitive)
  1845→			upperCode := strings.ToUpper(code)
  1846→			if upperCode == QuitCommandFull || upperCode == QuitCommandShort || upperCode == ExitCommand {
  1847→				slog.Info("Login cancelled by user")
  1848→				analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventLoginCancelled, nil)
  1849→				fmt.Println()
  1850→				fmt.Println("👋 Login cancelled.")
  1851→				fmt.Println()
  1852→				return nil
  1853→			}
  1854→
  1855→			// Normalize and validate the device code
  1856→			normalizedCode, valid := normalizeDeviceCode(code)
  1857→			if !valid {
  1858→				invalidAttempts++
  1859→				slog.Debug("Invalid code format entered", "original", code, "attempt", invalidAttempts)
  1860→
  1861→				if invalidAttempts >= maxAttempts {
  1862→					analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventLoginFailed, analytics.Properties{
  1863→						"error": "max_attempts_exceeded",
  1864→						"stage": "code_validation",
  1865→					})
  1866→					return fmt.Errorf("maximum login attempts exceeded")
  1867→				}
  1868→
  1869→				fmt.Println()
  1870→				fmt.Println("❌ Invalid code format. The code should be 6 alphanumeric characters.")
  1871→				fmt.Println("   Examples: Ab1c23 or Ab1-c23")
  1872→				fmt.Println()
  1873→				continue // Try again
  1874→			}
  1875→
  1876→			// Valid code format - proceed with authentication
  1877→			// Format the code for display (add dash back for readability)
  1878→			displayCode := normalizedCode[:3] + "-" + normalizedCode[3:]
  1879→			slog.Info("Valid authentication code received", "code", displayCode)
  1880→
  1881→			fmt.Println()
  1882→			fmt.Printf("✅ Code received: %s\n", displayCode)
  1883→			fmt.Println("🔄 Authenticating...")
  1884→			fmt.Println()
  1885→
  1886→			// Exchange code for authentication tokens
  1887→			if err := cloud.LoginWithDeviceCode(normalizedCode); err != nil {
  1888→				invalidAttempts++
  1889→				slog.Error("Failed to authenticate with device code", "error", err)
  1890→				analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventLoginFailed, analytics.Properties{
  1891→					"error": err.Error(),
  1892→					"stage": "device_login",
  1893→				})
  1894→
  1895→				// Check if it's an invalid code error
  1896→				if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "invalid") || strings.Contains(err.Error(), "expired") {
  1897→					if invalidAttempts >= maxAttempts {
  1898→						analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventLoginFailed, analytics.Properties{
  1899→							"error": "max_attempts_exceeded",
  1900→							"stage": "device_login",
  1901→						})
  1902→						return fmt.Errorf("maximum login attempts exceeded")
  1903→					}
  1904→
  1905→					fmt.Println("❌ Authentication failed: " + err.Error())
  1906→					fmt.Println()
  1907→					fmt.Println("Please try entering the code again.")
  1908→					fmt.Println()
  1909→					continue // Let user try again
  1910→				}
  1911→
  1912→				// Other errors are fatal
  1913→				return fmt.Errorf("authentication failed: %w", err)
  1914→			}
  1915→
  1916→			// Get user details to show who they logged in as
  1917→			username, _ := cloud.AuthenticatedAs()
  1918→
  1919→			fmt.Println("🎉 Success! You're now logged in to SpecStory Cloud!")
  1920→			if username != "" {
  1921→				fmt.Printf("👤 Logged in as: %s\n", username)
  1922→			}
  1923→			fmt.Println()
  1924→			fmt.Println("🚀 Ready to sync your sessions to SpecStory Cloud! 💪")
  1925→			fmt.Println("   • specstory run  - Launch terminal coding agents with auto-sync'ing")
  1926→			fmt.Println("   • specstory sync - Sync markdown files for existing sessions")
  1927→			fmt.Println()
  1928→
  1929→			analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventLoginSuccess, analytics.Properties{
  1930→				"user":   username,
  1931→				"method": "device_code",
  1932→			})
  1933→			slog.Info("Login flow completed successfully", "user", username)
  1934→			return nil
  1935→		}
  1936→	},
  1937→}
  1938→
  1939→// Command to log out from SpecStory Cloud
  1940→var logoutCmd = &cobra.Command{
  1941→	Use:   "logout",
  1942→	Short: "Log out from SpecStory Cloud",
  1943→	Long:  `Log out from SpecStory Cloud by removing authentication credentials.`,
  1944→	Example: `
  1945→# Log out
  1946→specstory logout
  1947→
  1948→# Log out without a confirmation prompt
  1949→specstory logout --force`,
  1950→	RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
  1951→		slog.Info("Running logout command")
  1952→
  1953→		// Check if user is authenticated
  1954→		if !cloud.IsAuthenticated() {
  1955→			// Not logged in - but still clean up any auth file that might exist
  1956→			_ = cloud.Logout() // Always attempt to remove the file
  1957→
  1958→			// Not logged in - be friendly and funky!
  1959→			fmt.Println()
  1960→			fmt.Println("🎉 Good news! You're not logged in!")
  1961→			fmt.Println("✨ Nothing to log out from - All is well in the universe! 💫")
  1962→			fmt.Println()
  1963→			return nil
  1964→		}
  1965→
  1966→		// Get the force flag value
  1967→		force, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("force")
  1968→
  1969→		// If not forcing, ask for confirmation
  1970→		if !force {
  1971→			// User is logged in - ask for confirmation with style
  1972→			fmt.Println()
  1973→			fmt.Println("🔐 You're currently logged in to SpecStory Cloud.")
  1974→			fmt.Println("🤔 Are you sure you want to log out?")
  1975→			fmt.Println()
  1976→			fmt.Print("Type 'yes' to confirm logout, or anything else to stay connected: ")
  1977→
  1978→			// Read user input
  1979→			reader := bufio.NewReader(os.Stdin)
  1980→			input, err := reader.ReadString('\n')
  1981→			if err != nil {
  1982→				slog.Error("Failed to read user input", "error", err)
  1983→				return fmt.Errorf("failed to read confirmation: %w", err)
  1984→			}
  1985→
  1986→			// Trim whitespace and convert to lowercase
  1987→			input = strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(input))
  1988→
  1989→			if input != "yes" {
  1990→				// User chose not to logout - celebrate their decision!
  1991→				fmt.Println()
  1992→				fmt.Println("🎊 Awesome! You're staying connected!")
  1993→				fmt.Println("🚀 Keep on building amazing things! 💪")
  1994→				fmt.Println()
  1995→				return nil
  1996→			}
  1997→		}
  1998→
  1999→		// Proceed with logout
  2000→		if err := cloud.Logout(); err != nil {
  2001→			slog.Error("Logout failed", "error", err)
  2002→			// Track logout error
  2003→			analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventLogout, analytics.Properties{
  2004→				"success": false,
  2005→				"forced":  force,
  2006→				"error":   err.Error(),
  2007→			})
  2008→			fmt.Println()
  2009→			fmt.Println("❌ Oops! Something went wrong during logout:")
  2010→			fmt.Printf("   %v\n", err)
  2011→			fmt.Println()
  2012→			return err
  2013→		}
  2014→
  2015→		// Track successful logout
  2016→		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventLogout, analytics.Properties{
  2017→			"success": true,
  2018→			"forced":  force,
  2019→		})
  2020→
  2021→		// Success!
  2022→		fmt.Println()
  2023→		fmt.Println("👋 Successfully logged out from SpecStory Cloud.")
  2024→		fmt.Println("💝 Thanks for using SpecStory! See you again soon! ✨")
  2025→		fmt.Println()
  2026→
  2027→		return nil
  2028→	},
  2029→}
  2030→
  2031→// Custom help command, needed over built-in help command to display our logo
  2032→var helpCmd = &cobra.Command{
  2033→	Use:     "help [command]",
  2034→	Aliases: []string{"h"},
  2035→	Short:   "Help about any command",
  2036→	Run: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
  2037→		// Track help command usage with context about why help was shown
  2038→		helpTopic := "general"
  2039→		helpReason := "requested"
  2040→
  2041→		// If a subcommand is specified, determine if it's valid
  2042→		if len(args) > 0 {
  2043→			targetCmd, _, err := rootCmd.Find(args)
  2044→			if err != nil {
  2045→				// Unknown command - track as error-triggered help
  2046→				helpReason = "unknown_command"
  2047→				fmt.Printf("Unknown command: %s\n", args[0])
  2048→				displayLogoAndHelp(rootCmd)
  2049→			} else {
  2050→				// Valid command - track the specific topic
  2051→				helpTopic = args[0]
  2052→				displayLogoAndHelp(targetCmd)
  2053→			}
  2054→		} else {
  2055→			// No command specified - general help requested
  2056→			displayLogoAndHelp(rootCmd)
  2057→		}
  2058→
  2059→		// Track analytics after determining the context
  2060→		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventHelpCommand, analytics.Properties{
  2061→			"help_topic":  helpTopic,
  2062→			"help_reason": helpReason,
  2063→		})
  2064→	},
  2065→}
  2066→
  2067→// Main entry point for the CLI
  2068→func main() {
  2069→	// Parse critical flags early by manually checking os.Args
  2070→	// This is necessary because cobra's ParseFlags doesn't work correctly before subcommands are added
  2071→	for _, arg := range os.Args[1:] {
  2072→		switch arg {
  2073→		case "--no-usage-analytics":
  2074→			noAnalytics = true
  2075→		case "--console":
  2076→			console = true
  2077→		case "--log":
  2078→			logFile = true
  2079→		case "--debug":
  2080→			debug = true
  2081→		case "--silent":
  2082→			silent = true
  2083→		case "--no-version-check":
  2084→			noVersionCheck = true
  2085→		}
  2086→		// Handle --output-dir=value format
  2087→		if strings.HasPrefix(arg, "--output-dir=") {
  2088→			outputDir = strings.TrimPrefix(arg, "--output-dir=")
  2089→		}
  2090→	}
  2091→
  2092→	// Set up logging early before creating commands (which access the registry)
  2093→	if console || logFile {
  2094→		var logPath string
  2095→		if logFile {
  2096→			config, _ := utils.SetupOutputConfig(outputDir)
  2097→			logPath = config.GetLogPath()
  2098→		}
  2099→		_ = log.SetupLogger(console, logFile, debug, logPath)
  2100→	} else {
  2101→		// Set up discard logger to prevent default slog output
  2102→		_ = log.SetupLogger(false, false, false, "")
  2103→	}
  2104→
  2105→	// NOW create the commands - after logging is configured
  2106→	rootCmd = createRootCommand()
  2107→	runCmd = createRunCommand()
  2108→	watchCmd = createWatchCommand()
  2109→	syncCmd = createSyncCommand()
  2110→	listCmd := cmd.CreateListCommand()
  2111→	checkCmd = createCheckCommand()
  2112→
  2113→	// Set version for the automatic version flag
  2114→	rootCmd.Version = version
  2115→
  2116→	// Override the default version template to match our version command output
  2117→	rootCmd.SetVersionTemplate("{{.Version}} (SpecStory)")
  2118→
  2119→	// Set our custom help command (for "specstory help")
  2120→	rootCmd.SetHelpCommand(helpCmd)
  2121→
  2122→	// Add the subcommands
  2123→	rootCmd.AddCommand(runCmd)
  2124→	rootCmd.AddCommand(watchCmd)
  2125→	rootCmd.AddCommand(syncCmd)
  2126→	rootCmd.AddCommand(listCmd)
  2127→	rootCmd.AddCommand(versionCmd)
  2128→	rootCmd.AddCommand(checkCmd)
  2129→	rootCmd.AddCommand(loginCmd)
  2130→	rootCmd.AddCommand(logoutCmd)
  2131→
  2132→	// Global flags available on all commands
  2133→	rootCmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVar(&console, "console", false, "enable error/warn/info output to stdout")
  2134→	rootCmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVar(&logFile, "log", false, "write error/warn/info output to ./.specstory/debug/debug.log")
  2135→	rootCmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVar(&debug, "debug", false, "enable debug-level output (requires --console or --log)")
  2136→	rootCmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVar(&noAnalytics, "no-usage-analytics", false, "disable usage analytics")
  2137→	rootCmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVar(&silent, "silent", false, "suppress all non-error output")
  2138→	rootCmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVar(&noVersionCheck, "no-version-check", false, "skip checking for newer versions")
  2139→	rootCmd.PersistentFlags().StringVar(&cloudToken, "cloud-token", "", "use a SpecStory Cloud refresh token for this session (bypasses login)")
  2140→	_ = rootCmd.PersistentFlags().MarkHidden("cloud-token") // Hidden flag
  2141→
  2142→	// Command-specific flags
  2143→	syncCmd.Flags().StringSliceP("session", "s", []string{}, "optional session IDs to sync (can be specified multiple times, provider-specific format)")
  2144→	syncCmd.Flags().StringVar(&outputDir, "output-dir", "", "custom output directory for markdown and debug files (default: ./.specstory/history)")
  2145→	syncCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&noCloudSync, "no-cloud-sync", false, "disable cloud sync functionality")
  2146→	syncCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&onlyCloudSync, "only-cloud-sync", false, "skip local markdown file saves, only upload to cloud (requires authentication)")
  2147→	syncCmd.Flags().StringVar(&cloudURL, "cloud-url", "", "override the default cloud API base URL")
  2148→	_ = syncCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("cloud-url") // Hidden flag
  2149→	syncCmd.Flags().Bool("debug-raw", false, "debug mode to output pretty-printed raw data files")
  2150→	_ = syncCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("debug-raw") // Hidden flag
  2151→	syncCmd.Flags().BoolP("local-time-zone", "", false, "use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (when not present: UTC)")
  2152→
  2153→	runCmd.Flags().StringP("command", "c", "", "custom agent execution command for the provider")
  2154→	runCmd.Flags().String("resume", "", "resume a specific session by ID")
  2155→	runCmd.Flags().StringVar(&outputDir, "output-dir", "", "custom output directory for markdown and debug files (default: ./.specstory/history)")
  2156→	runCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&noCloudSync, "no-cloud-sync", false, "disable cloud sync functionality")
  2157→	runCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&onlyCloudSync, "only-cloud-sync", false, "skip local markdown file saves, only upload to cloud (requires authentication)")
  2158→	runCmd.Flags().StringVar(&cloudURL, "cloud-url", "", "override the default cloud API base URL")
  2159→	_ = runCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("cloud-url") // Hidden flag
  2160→	runCmd.Flags().Bool("debug-raw", false, "debug mode to output pretty-printed raw data files")
  2161→	_ = runCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("debug-raw") // Hidden flag
  2162→	runCmd.Flags().BoolP("local-time-zone", "", false, "use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (when not present: UTC)")
  2163→
  2164→	watchCmd.Flags().StringVar(&outputDir, "output-dir", "", "custom output directory for markdown and debug files (default: ./.specstory/history)")
  2165→	watchCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&noCloudSync, "no-cloud-sync", false, "disable cloud sync functionality")
  2166→	watchCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&onlyCloudSync, "only-cloud-sync", false, "skip local markdown file saves, only upload to cloud (requires authentication)")
  2167→	watchCmd.Flags().StringVar(&cloudURL, "cloud-url", "", "override the default cloud API base URL")
  2168→	_ = watchCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("cloud-url") // Hidden flag
  2169→	watchCmd.Flags().Bool("debug-raw", false, "debug mode to output pretty-printed raw data files")
  2170→	_ = watchCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("debug-raw") // Hidden flag
  2171→	watchCmd.Flags().BoolP("local-time-zone", "", false, "use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (when not present: UTC)")
  2172→
  2173→	checkCmd.Flags().StringP("command", "c", "", "custom agent execution command for the provider")
  2174→
  2175→	logoutCmd.Flags().Bool("force", false, "skip confirmation prompt and logout immediately")
  2176→	logoutCmd.Flags().StringVar(&cloudURL, "cloud-url", "", "override the default cloud API base URL")
  2177→	_ = logoutCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("cloud-url") // Hidden flag
  2178→
  2179→	loginCmd.Flags().StringVar(&cloudURL, "cloud-url", "", "override the default cloud API base URL")
  2180→	_ = loginCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("cloud-url") // Hidden flag
  2181→
  2182→	// Initialize analytics with the full CLI command (unless disabled)
  2183→	slog.Debug("Analytics initialization check", "noAnalytics", noAnalytics, "flag_should_disable", noAnalytics)
  2184→	if !noAnalytics {
  2185→		slog.Debug("Initializing analytics")
  2186→		fullCommand := strings.Join(os.Args, " ")
  2187→		if err := analytics.Init(fullCommand, version); err != nil {
  2188→			// Log error but don't fail - analytics should not break the app
  2189→			slog.Warn("Failed to initialize analytics", "error", err)
  2190→		}
  2191→		defer func() { _ = analytics.Close() }() // Analytics errors shouldn't break the app
  2192→	} else {
  2193→		slog.Debug("Analytics disabled by --no-usage-analytics flag")
  2194→	}
  2195→
  2196→	// Check for updates (blocking)
  2197→	utils.CheckForUpdates(version, noVersionCheck, silent)
  2198→
  2199→	// Ensure proper cleanup and logging on exit
  2200→	defer func() {
  2201→		if r := recover(); r != nil {
  2202→			slog.Error("=== SpecStory PANIC ===", "panic", r)
  2203→			// Still try to wait for cloud sync even on panic
  2204→			_ = cloud.Shutdown(cloud.CloudSyncTimeout)
  2205→			log.CloseLogger()
  2206→			panic(r) // Re-panic after logging
  2207→		}
  2208→		// Wait for cloud sync operations to complete before exiting
  2209→		cloudStats := cloud.Shutdown(cloud.CloudSyncTimeout)
  2210→
  2211→		// Track cloud sync analytics if we have stats
  2212→		if cloudStats != nil {
  2213→			// Calculate total attempted (all sessions that started sync)
  2214→			totalCloudSessions := cloudStats.SessionsAttempted
  2215→
  2216→			// Track cloud sync completion event
  2217→			if cloudStats.SessionsUpdated > 0 || cloudStats.SessionsCreated > 0 || cloudStats.SessionsErrored > 0 || cloudStats.SessionsTimedOut > 0 {
  2218→				analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCloudSyncComplete, analytics.Properties{
  2219→					"sessions_created":   cloudStats.SessionsCreated,
  2220→					"sessions_updated":   cloudStats.SessionsUpdated,
  2221→					"sessions_skipped":   cloudStats.SessionsSkipped,
  2222→					"sessions_errored":   cloudStats.SessionsErrored,
  2223→					"sessions_timed_out": cloudStats.SessionsTimedOut,
  2224→					"total":              totalCloudSessions,
  2225→				})
  2226→			}
  2227→
  2228→			// Display cloud sync stats if not in silent mode
  2229→			if !silent && totalCloudSessions > 0 {
  2230→				fmt.Println() // Visual separation from provider sync output
  2231→
  2232→				// Determine if sync was complete or incomplete based on errors/timeouts
  2233→				if cloudStats.SessionsErrored > 0 || cloudStats.SessionsTimedOut > 0 {
  2234→					fmt.Printf("❌ Cloud sync incomplete! 📊 %s%d%s %s processed\n",
  2235→						log.ColorBoldCyan, totalCloudSessions, log.ColorReset, pluralSession(int(totalCloudSessions)))
  2236→				} else {
  2237→					fmt.Printf("☁️  Cloud sync complete! 📊 %s%d%s %s processed\n",
  2238→						log.ColorBoldCyan, totalCloudSessions, log.ColorReset, pluralSession(int(totalCloudSessions)))
  2239→				}
  2240→				fmt.Println()
  2241→
  2242→				fmt.Printf("  ⏭️ %s%d %s up to date (skipped)%s\n",
  2243→					log.ColorCyan, cloudStats.SessionsSkipped, pluralSession(int(cloudStats.SessionsSkipped)), log.ColorReset)
  2244→				fmt.Printf("  ♻️ %s%d %s updated%s\n",
  2245→					log.ColorYellow, cloudStats.SessionsUpdated, pluralSession(int(cloudStats.SessionsUpdated)), log.ColorReset)
  2246→				fmt.Printf("  ✨ %s%d new %s created%s\n",
  2247→					log.ColorGreen, cloudStats.SessionsCreated, pluralSession(int(cloudStats.SessionsCreated)), log.ColorReset)
  2248→
  2249→				// Only show errors if there were any
  2250→				if cloudStats.SessionsErrored > 0 {
  2251→					fmt.Printf("  ❌ %s%d %s errored%s\n",
  2252→						log.ColorRed, cloudStats.SessionsErrored, pluralSession(int(cloudStats.SessionsErrored)), log.ColorReset)
  2253→				}
  2254→
  2255→				// Only show timed out sessions if there were any
  2256→				if cloudStats.SessionsTimedOut > 0 {
  2257→					fmt.Printf("  ⏱️  %s%d %s timed out%s\n",
  2258→						log.ColorRed, cloudStats.SessionsTimedOut, pluralSession(int(cloudStats.SessionsTimedOut)), log.ColorReset)
  2259→				}
  2260→				fmt.Println()
  2261→
  2262→				// Display link to SpecStory Cloud (deep link to session if from run command)
  2263→				cwd, cwdErr := os.Getwd()
  2264→				if cwdErr == nil {
  2265→					identityManager := utils.NewProjectIdentityManager(cwd)
  2266→					if projectID, err := identityManager.GetProjectID(); err == nil {
  2267→						fmt.Printf("💡 Search and chat with your AI conversation history at:\n")
  2268→						if lastRunSessionID != "" {
  2269→							// Deep link to the specific session from run command
  2270→							fmt.Printf("   %shttps://cloud.specstory.com/projects/%s/sessions/%s%s\n\n",
  2271→								log.ColorBoldCyan, projectID, lastRunSessionID, log.ColorReset)
  2272→						} else {
  2273→							// Link to project overview for sync command
  2274→							fmt.Printf("   %shttps://cloud.specstory.com/projects/%s%s\n\n",
  2275→								log.ColorBoldCyan, projectID, log.ColorReset)
  2276→						}
  2277→					}
  2278→				}
  2279→			}
  2280→		}
  2281→
  2282→		if console || logFile {
  2283→			slog.Info("=== SpecStory Exiting ===", "code", 0, "status", "normal termination")
  2284→		}
  2285→		log.CloseLogger()
  2286→	}()
  2287→
  2288→	if err := fang.Execute(context.Background(), rootCmd, fang.WithVersion(version)); err != nil {
  2289→		// Check if we're running the check command by looking at the executed command
  2290→		executedCmd, _, _ := rootCmd.Find(os.Args[1:])
  2291→		if executedCmd == checkCmd {
  2292→			if console || logFile {
  2293→				slog.Error("=== SpecStory Exiting ===", "code", 2, "status", "agent execution failure")
  2294→				slog.Error("Error", "error", err)
  2295→			}
  2296→			// For check command, the error details are handled by checkSingleProvider/checkAllProviders
  2297→			// So we just exit silently here
  2298→			_ = cloud.Shutdown(cloud.CloudSyncTimeout)
  2299→			os.Exit(2)
  2300→		} else {
  2301→			if console || logFile {
  2302→				slog.Error("=== SpecStory Exiting ===", "code", 1, "status", "error")
  2303→				slog.Error("Error", "error", err)
  2304→			}
  2305→			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr) // Visual separation makes error output more noticeable
  2306→
  2307→			// Only show usage for actual command/flag errors from Cobra
  2308→			// These are errors like "unknown command", "unknown flag", "invalid argument", etc.
  2309→			// For all other errors (authentication, network, file system, etc.), we should NOT show usage
  2310→			errMsg := err.Error()
  2311→			isCommandError := strings.Contains(errMsg, "unknown command") ||
  2312→				strings.Contains(errMsg, "unknown flag") ||
  2313→				strings.Contains(errMsg, "invalid argument") ||
  2314→				strings.Contains(errMsg, "required flag") ||
  2315→				strings.Contains(errMsg, "accepts") || // e.g., "accepts 1 arg(s), received 2"
  2316→				strings.Contains(errMsg, "no such flag") ||
  2317→				strings.Contains(errMsg, "flag needs an argument")
  2318→
  2319→			if isCommandError {
  2320→				_ = rootCmd.Usage() // Ignore error; we're exiting anyway
  2321→				fmt.Println()       // Add visual separation after usage for better CLI readability
  2322→			}
  2323→			_ = cloud.Shutdown(cloud.CloudSyncTimeout)
  2324→			os.Exit(1)
  2325→		}
  2326→	}
  2327→}
  2328→
```

# Memory

## Lessons Learned

- **DRY is in CLAUDE.md** — Don't duplicate code blocks across command handlers. Extract helper functions on the first pass, not after being told. The CLAUDE.md explicitly says "Emphasize DRY code, look for existing code that can be reused, don't just write new code first."

- `pkg/provenance/` contains the correlation engine (moved from ai-provenance-lib). Files: types.go, engine.go, store.go, logging.go + tests. 20 tests total.
- `--provenance` flag is on `run` and `watch` commands, wired via `startProvenanceEngine()` helper in main.go.
- Planning doc: `docs/AI-PROVENANCE.md` — merged from ai-provenance-lib's PROVENANCE-EXTRACT.md and the original CLI planning doc.
- ai-provenance-lib source is at `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/ai-provenance-lib/` (NOT under getspecstory/).
- Intent is at `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/intent/` and imports specstory-cli as a library.

The user wants to proceed to Phase 2: Extracting & Pushing Agent Events. Let me re-read the plan from the AI-PROVENANCE.md to understand what needs to be done.

From the plan:

### Phase 2: Extracting & Pushing Agent Events

**Goal:** When provenance is enabled, extract file-modifying tool uses from SessionData and push them as AgentEvents.

**Steps:**

1. Create `ExtractAgentEvents(sessionData *schema.SessionData) []provenance.AgentEvent`
   - Iterates exchanges → messages
   - For each message with a file-modifying tool type AND PathHints, creates one AgentEvent per PathHint
   - Only `write`, `shell`, and `generic` tools generate events when PathHints are present; `read`, `search`, `task`, and `unknown` are skipped
   - Event IDs are deterministically generated using SHA-256 hashing of the sessionID, exchangeID, messageID, and path combined
   - Tool types map to change types (with `write` mapping to `"write"` for now, though this could be refined later to distinguish creates from edits)
   - The AgentType comes from `sessionData.Provider.ID` and AgentModel from `message.Model`
   - PathHints are accessed directly from the Message object

Now I need to implement the function that pushes these events to the provenance engine, collecting any resulting ProvenanceRecords and logging each operation at the appropriate level. I'll also need to wire this into the session callback in main.go, and I should create a new file `pkg/provenance/extract.go` for this logic. Let me verify the exact field names and structure of the SessionData types to ensure I'm accessing everything correctly. I need to parse the Message timestamp from RFC3339 format to time.Time for the AgentEvent. I'm planning to create `ExtractAgentEvents` and `PushAgentEvents` functions in a new `pkg/provenance/extract.go` file to bridge the CLI schema types with the provenance engine. But I'm concerned about adding a dependency from provenance to the schema package—that breaks the current isolation of the provenance package.

I'm weighing three options: putting the extraction logic in provenance (creates coupling), moving it to main.go (keeps provenance clean), or creating a separate `pkg/provenance/cli/` subpackage for clean separation. I think option 1 is actually fine since the extraction is specifically about converting CLI schema types into provenance types, and it's a one-way dependency that doesn't compromise the core engine.

Now I need to look at the schema types to get the exact field names and types I'll be working with.
</details></think>

     9→	RoleUser  = "user"
    10→	RoleAgent = "agent"
    11→)
    12→
    13→// ContentType constants for content parts
    14→const (
    15→	ContentTypeText     = "text"
    16→	ContentTypeThinking = "thinking"
    17→)

    20→const (

    22→	ToolTypeRead    = "read"
    23→	ToolTypeSearch  = "search"
    24→	ToolTypeShell   = "shell"
    25→	ToolTypeTask    = "task"
    26→	ToolTypeGeneric = "generic"
    27→	ToolTypeUnknown = "unknown"
    28→)
    29→
    30→// SessionData is the unified data format for sessions from all terminal coding agent providers
    31→type SessionData struct {
    32→	SchemaVersion string       `json:"schemaVersion"`
    33→	Provider      ProviderInfo `json:"provider"`
    34→	SessionID     string       `json:"sessionId"`
    35→	CreatedAt     string       `json:"createdAt"`
    36→	UpdatedAt     string       `json:"updatedAt,omitempty"`
    37→	Slug          string       `json:"slug,omitempty"`
    38→	WorkspaceRoot string       `json:"workspaceRoot"`
    39→	Exchanges     []Exchange   `json:"exchanges"`
    40→}
    41→
    42→// ProviderInfo is the information about the agent provider that created the session
    43→type ProviderInfo struct {
    44→	ID      string `json:"id"`
    45→	Name    string `json:"name"`
    46→	Version string `json:"version"`
    47→}
    48→
    49→// Exchange is a conversational turn with ordered messages
    50→type Exchange struct {
    51→	ExchangeID string                 `json:"exchangeId"`
    52→	StartTime  string                 `json:"startTime,omitempty"`
    53→	EndTime    string                 `json:"endTime,omitempty"`
    54→	Messages   []Message              `json:"messages"`
    55→	Metadata   map[string]interface{} `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
    56→}
    57→
    58→// Message is either a user or agent message
    59→type Message struct {
    60→	ID        string                 `json:"id,omitempty"`
    61→	Timestamp string                 `json:"timestamp,omitempty"`
    62→	Role      string                 `json:"role"` // "user" or "agent"
    63→	Model     string                 `json:"model,omitempty"`
    64→	Content   []ContentPart          `json:"content,omitempty"`
    65→	Tool      *ToolInfo              `json:"tool,omitempty"`
    66→	PathHints []string               `json:"pathHints,omitempty"`
    67→	Metadata  map[string]interface{} `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
    68→}
    69→
    70→// ContentPart is a content part (text or thinking)
    71→type ContentPart struct {
    72→	Type string `json:"type"` // "text" or "thinking"
    73→	Text string `json:"text"` // Content text (for both text and thinking)
    74→}
    75→
    76→// ToolInfo is tool use information
    77→type ToolInfo struct {
    78→	Name string `json:"name"` // Name of the tool
    79→
    80→	// Tool type classification:
    81→	// Use `write` for tools that write to the file system
    82→	// Use `read` for tools that read from the file system
    83→	// Use `search` for tools that search the file system or the web (e.g. `grep`, `glob`, `find`, `web_search` etc.)
    84→	// Use `shell` for tools that run shell commands (e.g. `bash`, `sed`, `awk`, `ls` etc.)
    85→	// Use `task` for tools that manage tasks (e.g. `todo`, `todo_write`, `todo_read`, `tasklist`, `task_manager` etc.)
    86→	// Use `generic` for tools that the provider knows about but that don't map well to a specific type
    87→	// Use `unknown` for tools that the provider does not know about
    88→	Type string `json:"type"` // Type of the tool: write, read, search, shell, task, generic, unknown
    89→
    90→	UseID             string                 `json:"useId,omitempty"`             // ID of the tool use
    91→	Input             map[string]interface{} `json:"input,omitempty"`             // Input to the tool
    92→	Output            map[string]interface{} `json:"output,omitempty"`            // Output from the tool
    93→	Summary           *string                `json:"summary,omitempty"`           // Summary content (will be wrapped in <summary> tags by CLI)
    94→	FormattedMarkdown *string                `json:"formattedMarkdown,omitempty"` // Pre-formatted markdown from provider (will be wrapped in <formatted-markdown> tags by CLI)
    95→}
    96→
    97→// Validate checks the SessionData against schema constraints and logs warnings for any issues.
    98→// Returns true if validation passed, false if there were any issues.
    99→func (s *SessionData) Validate() bool {
   100→	valid := true
   101→
   102→	// Check schema version
   103→	if s.SchemaVersion != "1.0" {
   104→		slog.Warn("schema validation: schemaVersion must be '1.0'", "got", s.SchemaVersion)
   105→		valid = false
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	// Check required root fields
   109→	if s.Provider.ID == "" {
   110→		slog.Warn("schema validation: provider.id is required")
   111→		valid = false
   112→	}
   113→	if s.Provider.Name == "" {
   114→		slog.Warn("schema validation: provider.name is required")
   115→		valid = false
   116→	}
   117→	if s.Provider.Version == "" {
   118→		slog.Warn("schema validation: provider.version is required")
   119→		valid = false
   120→	}
   121→	if s.SessionID == "" {
   122→		slog.Warn("schema validation: sessionId is required")
   123→		valid = false
   124→	}
   125→	if s.CreatedAt == "" {
   126→		slog.Warn("schema validation: createdAt is required")
   127→		valid = false
   128→	}
   129→	if s.WorkspaceRoot == "" {
   130→		slog.Warn("schema validation: workspaceRoot is required")
   131→		valid = false
   132→	}
   133→
   134→	// Validate each exchange
   135→	for i, exchange := range s.Exchanges {
   136→		if exchange.ExchangeID == "" {
   137→			slog.Warn("schema validation: exchange.exchangeId is required", "exchangeIndex", i)
   138→			valid = false
   139→		}
   140→
   141→		// Validate each message in the exchange
   142→		for j, msg := range exchange.Messages {
   143→			if !msg.validate(i, j) {
   144→				valid = false
   145→			}
   146→		}
   147→	}
   148→
   149→	return valid
   150→}
   151→
   152→// validate checks a single message against schema constraints.
   153→func (m *Message) validate(exchangeIndex, messageIndex int) bool {
   154→	valid := true
   155→	logCtx := []any{"exchangeIndex", exchangeIndex, "messageIndex", messageIndex}
   156→
   157→	// Check role is valid
   158→	if m.Role != RoleUser && m.Role != RoleAgent {
   159→		slog.Warn("schema validation: message.role must be 'user' or 'agent'",
   160→			append(logCtx, "got", m.Role)...)
   161→		valid = false
   162→	}
   163→
   164→	// User message constraints
   165→	if m.Role == RoleUser {
   166→		if len(m.Content) == 0 {
   167→			slog.Warn("schema validation: user message must have non-empty content", logCtx...)
   168→			valid = false
   169→		}
   170→		if m.Tool != nil {
   171→			slog.Warn("schema validation: user message cannot have tool", logCtx...)
   172→			valid = false
   173→		}
   174→		if m.Model != "" {
   175→			slog.Warn("schema validation: user message cannot have model", logCtx...)
   176→			valid = false
   177→		}
   178→	}
   179→
   180→	// Agent message constraints: must have at least one of content, tool, or pathHints
   181→	if m.Role == RoleAgent {
   182→		hasContent := len(m.Content) > 0
   183→		hasTool := m.Tool != nil
   184→		hasPathHints := len(m.PathHints) > 0
   185→		if !hasContent && !hasTool && !hasPathHints {
   186→			slog.Warn("schema validation: agent message must have content, tool, or pathHints", logCtx...)
   187→			valid = false
   188→		}
   189→	}
   190→
   191→	// Validate content parts
   192→	for k, part := range m.Content {
   193→		if !part.validate(exchangeIndex, messageIndex, k) {
   194→			valid = false
   195→		}
   196→	}
   197→
   198→	// Validate tool if present
   199→	if m.Tool != nil {
   200→		if !m.Tool.validate(exchangeIndex, messageIndex) {
   201→			valid = false
   202→		}
   203→	}
   204→
   205→	return valid
   206→}
   207→
   208→// validate checks a single content part against schema constraints.
   209→func (c *ContentPart) validate(exchangeIndex, messageIndex, partIndex int) bool {
   210→	valid := true
   211→	logCtx := []any{"exchangeIndex", exchangeIndex, "messageIndex", messageIndex, "partIndex", partIndex}
   212→
   213→	if c.Type != ContentTypeText && c.Type != ContentTypeThinking {
   214→		slog.Warn("schema validation: contentPart.type must be 'text' or 'thinking'",
   215→			append(logCtx, "got", c.Type)...)
   216→		valid = false
   217→	}
   218→
   219→	return valid
   220→}
   221→
   222→// validate checks tool info against schema constraints.
   223→func (t *ToolInfo) validate(exchangeIndex, messageIndex int) bool {
   224→	valid := true
   225→	logCtx := []any{"exchangeIndex", exchangeIndex, "messageIndex", messageIndex}
   226→
   227→	if t.Name == "" {
   228→		slog.Warn("schema validation: tool.name is required", logCtx...)
   229→		valid = false
   230→	}
   231→
   232→	validTypes := map[string]bool{
   233→		ToolTypeWrite:   true,
   234→		ToolTypeRead:    true,
   235→		ToolTypeSearch:  true,
   236→		ToolTypeShell:   true,
   237→		ToolTypeTask:    true,
   238→		ToolTypeGeneric: true,
   239→		ToolTypeUnknown: true,
   240→	}
   241→	if !validTypes[t.Type] {
   242→		slog.Warn("schema validation: tool.type must be one of: write, read, search, shell, task, generic, unknown",
   243→			append(logCtx, "got", t.Type)...)
   244→		valid = false
   245→	}
   246→
   247→	return valid
   248→}
   249→
```

```go
package provenance

import (
	"context"
	"crypto/sha256"
	"fmt"
	"log/slog"
	"time"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
)

// fileModifyingTools are tool types that can modify files and should generate agent events.
var fileModifyingTools = map[string]bool{
	schema.ToolTypeWrite:   true,
	schema.ToolTypeShell:   true,
	schema.ToolTypeGeneric: true,
}

// deterministicID generates a stable ID from the given components so that
// re-processing the same session produces the same events (deduped via INSERT OR IGNORE).
func deterministicID(parts ...string) string {
	h := sha256.New()
	for _, p := range parts {
		h.Write([]byte(p))
	}
	return fmt.Sprintf("%x", h.Sum(nil))
}

// parseTimestamp attempts to parse an RFC3339 timestamp string.
// Returns the parsed time or the zero value if parsing fails.
func parseTimestamp(ts string) time.Time {
	t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, ts)
	if err != nil {
		// Try RFC3339Nano as a fallback
		t, _ = time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, ts)
	}
	return t
}

// ExtractAgentEvents walks a SessionData's exchanges and messages, producing
// one AgentEvent per PathHint on file-modifying tool uses (write, shell, generic).
// Non-modifying tool types (read, search, task, unknown) are skipped.
func ExtractAgentEvents(sessionData *schema.SessionData) []AgentEvent {
	if sessionData == nil {
		return nil
	}

	var events []AgentEvent

	for _, exchange := range sessionData.Exchanges {
		for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
			if msg.Tool == nil || !fileModifyingTools[msg.Tool.Type] {
				continue
			}
			if len(msg.PathHints) == 0 {
				continue
			}

			ts := parseTimestamp(msg.Timestamp)
			// Fall back to exchange start time if message has no timestamp
			if ts.IsZero() {
				ts = parseTimestamp(exchange.StartTime)
			}

			for _, path := range msg.PathHints {
				events = append(events, AgentEvent{
					ID:         deterministicID(sessionData.SessionID, exchange.ExchangeID, msg.ID, path),
					FilePath:   path,
					ChangeType: msg.Tool.Type,
					Timestamp:  ts,
					SessionID:  sessionData.SessionID,
					ExchangeID: exchange.ExchangeID,
					MessageID:  msg.ID,
					AgentType:  sessionData.Provider.ID,
					AgentModel: msg.Model,
				})
			}
		}
	}

	return events
}

// PushAgentEvents pushes a slice of agent events to the engine and returns
// any ProvenanceRecords produced by matches. Errors on individual events
// are logged but do not stop processing.
func PushAgentEvents(ctx context.Context, engine *Engine, events []AgentEvent) []*ProvenanceRecord {
	var records []*ProvenanceRecord

	for _, event := range events {
		record, err := engine.PushAgentEvent(ctx, event)
		if err != nil {
			slog.Warn("Failed to push agent event",
				"eventID", event.ID,
				"filePath", event.FilePath,
				"error", err)
			continue
		}
		if record != nil {
			records = append(records, record)
		}
	}

	return records
}

// ProcessSessionEvents extracts agent events from session data, pushes them to
// the engine, and returns any resulting provenance records.
// Safe to call with a nil engine — returns nil immediately.
func ProcessSessionEvents(ctx context.Context, engine *Engine, sessionData *schema.SessionData) []*ProvenanceRecord {
	if engine == nil || sessionData == nil {
		return nil
	}

	events := ExtractAgentEvents(sessionData)
	if len(events) == 0 {
		return nil
	}

	slog.Debug("Extracted agent events from session",
		"sessionID", sessionData.SessionID,
		"eventCount", len(events))

	return PushAgentEvents(ctx, engine, events)
}

```

```go
package provenance

import (
	"context"
	"crypto/sha256"
	"fmt"
	"log/slog"
	"time"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
)

// fileModifyingTools are tool types that can modify files and should generate agent events.
var fileModifyingTools = map[string]bool{
	schema.ToolTypeWrite:   true,
	schema.ToolTypeShell:   true,
	schema.ToolTypeGeneric: true,
}

// deterministicID generates a stable ID from the given components so that
// re-processing the same session produces the same events (deduped via INSERT OR IGNORE).
func deterministicID(parts ...string) string {
	h := sha256.New()
	for _, p := range parts {
		h.Write([]byte(p))
	}
	return fmt.Sprintf("%x", h.Sum(nil))
}

// parseTimestamp attempts to parse an RFC3339 timestamp string.
// Returns the parsed time or the zero value if parsing fails.
func parseTimestamp(ts string) time.Time {
	t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, ts)
	if err != nil {
		// Try RFC3339Nano as a fallback
		t, _ = time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, ts)
	}
	return t
}

// ExtractAgentEvents walks a SessionData's exchanges and messages, producing
// one AgentEvent per PathHint on file-modifying tool uses (write, shell, generic).
// Non-modifying tool types (read, search, task, unknown) are skipped.
func ExtractAgentEvents(sessionData *schema.SessionData) []AgentEvent {
	if sessionData == nil {
		return nil
	}

	var events []AgentEvent

	for _, exchange := range sessionData.Exchanges {
		for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
			if msg.Tool == nil || !fileModifyingTools[msg.Tool.Type] {
				continue
			}
			if len(msg.PathHints) == 0 {
				continue
			}

			ts := parseTimestamp(msg.Timestamp)
			// Fall back to exchange start time if message has no timestamp
			if ts.IsZero() {
				ts = parseTimestamp(exchange.StartTime)
			}

			for _, path := range msg.PathHints {
				events = append(events, AgentEvent{
					ID:         deterministicID(sessionData.SessionID, exchange.ExchangeID, msg.ID, path),
					FilePath:   path,
					ChangeType: msg.Tool.Type,
					Timestamp:  ts,
					SessionID:  sessionData.SessionID,
					ExchangeID: exchange.ExchangeID,
					MessageID:  msg.ID,
					AgentType:  sessionData.Provider.ID,
					AgentModel: msg.Model,
				})
			}
		}
	}

	return events
}

// PushAgentEvents pushes a slice of agent events to the engine and returns
// any ProvenanceRecords produced by matches. Errors on individual events
// are logged but do not stop processing.
func PushAgentEvents(ctx context.Context, engine *Engine, events []AgentEvent) []*ProvenanceRecord {
	var records []*ProvenanceRecord

	for _, event := range events {
		record, err := engine.PushAgentEvent(ctx, event)
		if err != nil {
			slog.Warn("Failed to push agent event",
				"eventID", event.ID,
				"filePath", event.FilePath,
				"error", err)
			continue
		}
		if record != nil {
			records = append(records, record)
		}
	}

	return records
}

// ProcessSessionEvents extracts agent events from session data, pushes them to
// the engine, and returns any resulting provenance records.
// Safe to call with a nil engine — returns nil immediately.
func ProcessSessionEvents(ctx context.Context, engine *Engine, sessionData *schema.SessionData) []*ProvenanceRecord {
	if engine == nil || sessionData == nil {
		return nil
	}

	events := ExtractAgentEvents(sessionData)
	if len(events) == 0 {
		return nil
	}

	slog.Debug("Extracted agent events from session",
		"sessionID", sessionData.SessionID,
		"eventCount", len(events))

	return PushAgentEvents(ctx, engine, events)
}

```

package provenance

import (
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
)

func TestExtractAgentEvents(t *testing.T) {
	baseTime := time.Date(2025, 6, 15, 10, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
	baseTimeStr := baseTime.Format(time.RFC3339)

	tests := []struct {
		name          string
		sessionData   *schema.SessionData
		wantCount     int
		wantPaths     []string
		wantAgentType string
	}{
		{
			name:        "nil session data",
			sessionData: nil,
			wantCount:   0,
		},
		{
			name: "empty exchanges",
			sessionData: &schema.SessionData{
				SessionID: "sess-1",
				Provider:  schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "claude-code"},
				Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{},
			},
			wantCount: 0,
		},
		{
			name: "write tool with path hints produces events",
			sessionData: &schema.SessionData{
				SessionID: "sess-1",
				Provider:  schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "claude-code"},
				Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
					{
						ExchangeID: "ex-1",
						Messages: []schema.Message{
							{
								ID:        "msg-1",
								Timestamp: baseTimeStr,
								Role:      schema.RoleAgent,
								Model:     "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
								Tool:      &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Write", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite},
								PathHints: []string{"src/main.go"},
							},
						},
					},
				},
			},
			wantCount:     1,
			wantPaths:     []string{"src/main.go"},
			wantAgentType: "claude-code",
		},
		{
			name: "read tool produces no events",
			sessionData: &schema.SessionData{
				SessionID: "sess-1",
				Provider:  schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "claude-code"},
				Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
					{
						ExchangeID: "ex-1",
						Messages: []schema.Message{
							{
								ID:        "msg-1",
								Timestamp: baseTimeStr,
								Role:      schema.RoleAgent,
								Tool:      &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Read", Type: schema.ToolTypeRead},
								PathHints: []string{"src/main.go"},
							},
						},
					},
				},
			},
			wantCount: 0,
		},
		{
			name: "search tool produces no events",
			sessionData: &schema.SessionData{
				SessionID: "sess-1",
				Provider:  schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "claude-code"},
				Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
					{
						ExchangeID: "ex-1",
						Messages: []schema.Message{
							{
								ID:        "msg-1",
								Timestamp: baseTimeStr,
								Role:      schema.RoleAgent,
								Tool:      &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Grep", Type: schema.ToolTypeSearch},
								PathHints: []string{"src/main.go"},
							},
						},
					},
				},
			},
			wantCount: 0,
		},
		{
			name: "shell tool with path hints produces events",
			sessionData: &schema.SessionData{
				SessionID: "sess-1",
				Provider:  schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "claude-code"},
				Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
					{
						ExchangeID: "ex-1",
						Messages: []schema.Message{
							{
								ID:        "msg-1",
								Timestamp: baseTimeStr,
								Role:      schema.RoleAgent,
								Tool:      &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Bash", Type: schema.ToolTypeShell},
								PathHints: []string{"build/output.bin"},
							},
						},
					},
				},
			},
			wantCount: 1,
			wantPaths: []string{"build/output.bin"},
		},
		{
			name: "generic tool with path hints produces events",
			sessionData: &schema.SessionData{
				SessionID: "sess-1",
				Provider:  schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "cursor"},
				Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
					{
						ExchangeID: "ex-1",
						Messages: []schema.Message{
							{
								ID:        "msg-1",
								Timestamp: baseTimeStr,
								Role:      schema.RoleAgent,
								Tool:      &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "custom_tool", Type: schema.ToolTypeGeneric},
								PathHints: []string{"foo.txt"},
							},
						},
					},
				},
			},
			wantCount:     1,
			wantPaths:     []string{"foo.txt"},
			wantAgentType: "cursor",
		},
		{
			name: "write tool without path hints produces no events",
			sessionData: &schema.SessionData{
				SessionID: "sess-1",
				Provider:  schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "claude-code"},
				Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
					{
						ExchangeID: "ex-1",
						Messages: []schema.Message{
							{
								ID:        "msg-1",
								Timestamp: baseTimeStr,
								Role:      schema.RoleAgent,
								Tool:      &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Write", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite},
								PathHints: []string{},
							},
						},
					},
				},
			},
			wantCount: 0,
		},
		{
			name: "multiple path hints produce multiple events",
			sessionData: &schema.SessionData{
				SessionID: "sess-1",
				Provider:  schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "claude-code"},
				Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
					{
						ExchangeID: "ex-1",
						Messages: []schema.Message{
							{
								ID:        "msg-1",
								Timestamp: baseTimeStr,
								Role:      schema.RoleAgent,
								Tool:      &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Write", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite},
								PathHints: []string{"a.go", "b.go", "c.go"},
							},
						},
					},
				},
			},
			wantCount: 3,
			wantPaths: []string{"a.go", "b.go", "c.go"},
		},
		{
			name: "message without tool produces no events",
			sessionData: &schema.SessionData{
				SessionID: "sess-1",
				Provider:  schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "claude-code"},
				Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
					{
						ExchangeID: "ex-1",
						Messages: []schema.Message{
							{
								ID:        "msg-1",
								Timestamp: baseTimeStr,
								Role:      schema.RoleAgent,
								PathHints: []string{"src/main.go"},
							},
						},
					},
				},
			},
			wantCount: 0,
		},
	}

	for _, tt := range tests {
		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
			events := ExtractAgentEvents(tt.sessionData)
			if len(events) != tt.wantCount {
				t.Errorf("got %d events, want %d", len(events), tt.wantCount)
				return
			}
			for i, wantPath := range tt.wantPaths {
				if events[i].FilePath != wantPath {
					t.Errorf("event[%d].FilePath = %q, want %q", i, events[i].FilePath, wantPath)
				}
			}
			if tt.wantAgentType != "" && len(events) > 0 {
				if events[0].AgentType != tt.wantAgentType {
					t.Errorf("event[0].AgentType = %q, want %q", events[0].AgentType, tt.wantAgentType)
				}
			}
		})
	}
}

func TestExtractAgentEvents_DeterministicIDs(t *testing.T) {
	sessionData := &schema.SessionData{
		SessionID: "sess-1",
		Provider:  schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "claude-code"},
		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
			{
				ExchangeID: "ex-1",
				Messages: []schema.Message{
					{
						ID:        "msg-1",
						Timestamp: time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339),
						Role:      schema.RoleAgent,
						Tool:      &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Write", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite},
						PathHints: []string{"src/main.go"},
					},
				},
			},
		},
	}

	events1 := ExtractAgentEvents(sessionData)
	events2 := ExtractAgentEvents(sessionData)

	if len(events1) != 1 || len(events2) != 1 {
		t.Fatalf("expected 1 event each, got %d and %d", len(events1), len(events2))
	}

	if events1[0].ID != events2[0].ID {
		t.Errorf("IDs not deterministic: %q != %q", events1[0].ID, events2[0].ID)
	}
}

func TestExtractAgentEvents_TimestampFallback(t *testing.T) {
	exchangeTime := time.Date(2025, 6, 15, 10, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)

	sessionData := &schema.SessionData{
		SessionID: "sess-1",
		Provider:  schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "claude-code"},
		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
			{
				ExchangeID: "ex-1",
				StartTime:  exchangeTime.Format(time.RFC3339),
				Messages: []schema.Message{
					{
						ID:   "msg-1",
						Role: schema.RoleAgent,
						// No Timestamp on message — should fall back to exchange StartTime
						Tool:      &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Write", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite},
						PathHints: []string{"src/main.go"},
					},
				},
			},
		},
	}

	events := ExtractAgentEvents(sessionData)
	if len(events) != 1 {
		t.Fatalf("expected 1 event, got %d", len(events))
	}

	if !events[0].Timestamp.Equal(exchangeTime) {
		t.Errorf("timestamp = %v, want %v (exchange fallback)", events[0].Timestamp, exchangeTime)
	}
}

func TestExtractAgentEvents_FieldMapping(t *testing.T) {
	ts := time.Date(2025, 6, 15, 10, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)

	sessionData := &schema.SessionData{
		SessionID: "sess-42",
		Provider:  schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "claude-code", Name: "Claude Code"},
		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
			{
				ExchangeID: "ex-7",
				Messages: []schema.Message{
					{
						ID:        "msg-99",
						Timestamp: ts.Format(time.RFC3339),
						Role:      schema.RoleAgent,
						Model:     "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
						Tool:      &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Write", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite},
						PathHints: []string{"pkg/foo.go"},
					},
				},
			},
		},
	}

	events := ExtractAgentEvents(sessionData)
	if len(events) != 1 {
		t.Fatalf("expected 1 event, got %d", len(events))
	}

	e := events[0]
	if e.SessionID != "sess-42" {
		t.Errorf("SessionID = %q, want %q", e.SessionID, "sess-42")
	}
	if e.ExchangeID != "ex-7" {
		t.Errorf("ExchangeID = %q, want %q", e.ExchangeID, "ex-7")
	}
	if e.MessageID != "msg-99" {
		t.Errorf("MessageID = %q, want %q", e.MessageID, "msg-99")
	}
	if e.AgentType != "claude-code" {
		t.Errorf("AgentType = %q, want %q", e.AgentType, "claude-code")
	}
	if e.AgentModel != "claude-sonnet-4-20250514" {
		t.Errorf("AgentModel = %q, want %q", e.AgentModel, "claude-sonnet-4-20250514")
	}
	if e.FilePath != "pkg/foo.go" {
		t.Errorf("FilePath = %q, want %q", e.FilePath, "pkg/foo.go")
	}
	if e.ChangeType != schema.ToolTypeWrite {
		t.Errorf("ChangeType = %q, want %q", e.ChangeType, schema.ToolTypeWrite)
	}
	if !e.Timestamp.Equal(ts) {
		t.Errorf("Timestamp = %v, want %v", e.Timestamp, ts)
	}
}

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-10 23:33:54Z)**_

All tests pass (33 provenance tests now — 20 original + 13 new), zero lint issues. Phase 2 is complete.

**What was added:**

**`pkg/provenance/agent.go`** — three functions:
- `ExtractAgentEvents(sessionData)` — walks exchanges → messages, creates one `AgentEvent` per PathHint on file-modifying tool uses (write, shell, generic). Skips read/search/task/unknown. Uses deterministic SHA-256 IDs for dedup safety. Falls back to exchange start time if message has no timestamp.
- `PushAgentEvents(ctx, engine, events)` — pushes each event, collects provenance records, logs errors without stopping.
- `ProcessSessionEvents(ctx, engine, sessionData)` — combines both; nil-safe on engine so callers don't need guards.

**`pkg/provenance/agent_test.go`** — 13 tests covering:
- Nil/empty input, all tool types (write/read/search/shell/generic), missing PathHints, multiple PathHints
- Deterministic IDs (same input → same ID)
- Timestamp fallback (message → exchange)
- Full field mapping verification

**`main.go`** — `processProvenanceEvents()` helper called from both run and watch session callbacks (DRY), right after `processSingleSession`.

Let me capture all the specific code, files, and technical details.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - Move ai-provenance-lib (at `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/ai-provenance-lib/`) into specstory-cli as `pkg/provenance/`
   - Merge two planning documents (`docs/AI-PROVENANCE.md` and `ai-provenance-lib/docs/planning/PROVENANCE-EXTRACT.md`) into one comprehensive doc
   - Implement Phase 1: `--provenance` flag and engine lifecycle on `run` and `watch` commands
   - Implement Phase 2: Extract and push agent events from SessionData when provenance is enabled
   - Update the Files Summary table in `docs/AI-PROVENANCE.md` to reflect current state (agent.go instead of extract.go, remove test files, combine filter.go into fswatcher.go)

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Provenance correlation engine: matches filesystem changes to AI agent activity via path suffix matching + ±5s time window
   - SQLite storage with WAL mode at `~/.specstory/provenance/provenance.db` (centralized, not per-project)
   - `FileEvent` (absolute paths) and `AgentEvent` (may be relative) as inputs, `ProvenanceRecord` as output
   - Path suffix matching at `/` directory boundary with closest-timestamp tiebreaking
   - Deterministic IDs via SHA-256 for dedup safety (INSERT OR IGNORE)
   - SessionData schema: `SessionData.Exchanges[].Messages[].Tool.Type` and `Messages[].PathHints[]`
   - File-modifying tool types: `write`, `shell`, `generic`; Skip: `read`, `search`, `task`, `unknown`
   - `schema.ToolTypeWrite`, `schema.ToolTypeShell`, etc. constants from `pkg/spi/schema/types.go`
   - Go functional options pattern (`WithDBPath`, `WithMatchWindow`) for engine configuration
   - Cobra CLI framework with `spf13/cobra` for command definitions
   - `modernc.org/sqlite` pure Go SQLite driver (already in CLI's go.mod at v1.45.0)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`pkg/provenance/types.go`** (copied from ai-provenance-lib, gofmt-fixed)
     - Defines `FileEvent`, `AgentEvent`, `ProvenanceRecord`, `NormalizePath()`, validation methods with sentinel errors
     - Core types for the provenance system

   - **`pkg/provenance/engine.go`** (copied from ai-provenance-lib, gofmt-fixed)
     - `NewEngine()`, `PushFileEvent()`, `PushAgentEvent()`, `pathSuffixMatch()`, `findBestMatch()`, `buildRecord()`, `Close()`
     - Default DB path: `~/.specstory/provenance/provenance.db`, default match window: 5s

   - **`pkg/provenance/store.go`** (copied from ai-provenance-lib)
     - SQLite persistence with WAL mode, `OpenStore()`, Push/Query/SetMatchedWith methods
     - Uses `INSERT OR IGNORE` for idempotent inserts

   - **`pkg/provenance/logging.go`** (copied from ai-provenance-lib)
     - `provenanceLogger()` returns `slog.Logger` with `component=provenance` tag

   - **`pkg/provenance/agent.go`** (NEW - Phase 2)
     - Bridges `schema.SessionData` to provenance `AgentEvent` types
     ```go
     package provenance

     import (
     	"context"
     	"crypto/sha256"
     	"fmt"
     	"log/slog"
     	"time"
     	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
     )

     var fileModifyingTools = map[string]bool{
     	schema.ToolTypeWrite:   true,
     	schema.ToolTypeShell:   true,
     	schema.ToolTypeGeneric: true,
     }

     func deterministicID(parts ...string) string {
     	h := sha256.New()
     	for _, p := range parts {
     		h.Write([]byte(p))
     	}
     	return fmt.Sprintf("%x", h.Sum(nil))
     }

     func parseTimestamp(ts string) time.Time {
     	t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, ts)
     	if err != nil {
     		t, _ = time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, ts)
     	}
     	return t
     }

     func ExtractAgentEvents(sessionData *schema.SessionData) []AgentEvent { ... }
     func PushAgentEvents(ctx context.Context, engine *Engine, events []AgentEvent) []*ProvenanceRecord { ... }
     func ProcessSessionEvents(ctx context.Context, engine *Engine, sessionData *schema.SessionData) []*ProvenanceRecord { ... }
     ```

   - **`pkg/provenance/agent_test.go`** (NEW - Phase 2)
     - 13 tests: `TestExtractAgentEvents` (10 table-driven subtests), `TestExtractAgentEvents_DeterministicIDs`, `TestExtractAgentEvents_TimestampFallback`, `TestExtractAgentEvents_FieldMapping`

   - **`pkg/provenance/{types,engine,store}_test.go`** (copied from ai-provenance-lib)
     - 20 original tests (9 engine, 8 store, 3 types)

   - **`main.go`** (modified for Phase 1 and Phase 2)
     - Added import: `"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/provenance"`
     - Added flag variable: `var provenanceEnabled bool`
     - Added `startProvenanceEngine()` helper (DRY - shared by run and watch):
     ```go
     func startProvenanceEngine() (*provenance.Engine, func(), error) {
     	if !provenanceEnabled {
     		return nil, func() {}, nil
     	}
     	engine, err := provenance.NewEngine()
     	if err != nil {
     		return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to start provenance engine: %w", err)
     	}
     	cleanup := func() {
     		if err := engine.Close(); err != nil {
     			slog.Error("Failed to close provenance engine", "error", err)
     		}
     		slog.Info("Provenance engine stopped")
     	}
     	return engine, cleanup, nil
     }
     ```
     - Added `processProvenanceEvents()` helper (DRY - called from both session callbacks):
     ```go
     func processProvenanceEvents(engine *provenance.Engine, session *spi.AgentChatSession) {
     	if engine == nil || session == nil || session.SessionData == nil {
     		return
     	}
     	provenance.ProcessSessionEvents(context.Background(), engine, session.SessionData)
     }
     ```
     - Both `run` and `watch` command handlers call `startProvenanceEngine()` and `processProvenanceEvents()` in their session callbacks
     - `--provenance` flag registered on both `runCmd` and `watchCmd`

   - **`docs/AI-PROVENANCE.md`** (rewritten - merged planning doc)
     - Combined content from CLI's old AI-PROVENANCE.md and ai-provenance-lib's PROVENANCE-EXTRACT.md
     - Dropped: private dependency strategy, go.work, scoring system details
     - Added: architecture sections, matching algorithm, data flow diagram, implementation phases

   - **`pkg/spi/schema/types.go`** (read only, not modified)
     - Key types: `SessionData`, `Exchange`, `Message`, `ToolInfo`, `ProviderInfo`
     - `Message.PathHints []string`, `Message.Tool *ToolInfo`, `ToolInfo.Type string`
     - Tool type constants: `ToolTypeWrite`, `ToolTypeRead`, `ToolTypeSearch`, `ToolTypeShell`, `ToolTypeTask`, `ToolTypeGeneric`, `ToolTypeUnknown`

```text
   168→---
   169→
   170→### Phase 1: `--provenance` Flag & Engine Lifecycle
   171→
   172→**Goal:** `run` and `watch` accept `--provenance`. When set, a provenance Engine is created and cleanly shut down.
   173→
   174→**Steps:**
   175→
   176→1. Add `--provenance` (bool) flag on `runCmd` and `watchCmd` in `main.go`
   177→2. When `--provenance` is true, call `provenance.NewEngine()`
   178→3. Defer `engine.Close()` for cleanup
   179→4. Log engine creation and stop at INFO level
   180→
   181→**Key files:** `main.go`
   182→
   183→**Verification:**
   184→- `specstory run --provenance` starts normally, logs "Provenance engine started", creates DB at `~/.specstory/provenance/provenance.db`
   185→- `specstory run` (without flag) works unchanged
   186→- `specstory watch --provenance` same behavior
   187→
   188→---
   189→
   190→### Phase 2: Extracting & Pushing Agent Events
   191→
   192→**Goal:** When provenance is enabled, extract file-modifying tool uses from SessionData and push them as AgentEvents.
   193→
   194→**Steps:**
   195→
   196→1. Create `ExtractAgentEvents(sessionData *schema.SessionData) []provenance.AgentEvent`
   197→   - Iterates exchanges → messages
   198→   - For each message with a file-modifying tool type AND PathHints, creates one AgentEvent per PathHint
   199→   - **Tool types that generate events:** `write`, `shell`, `generic` (when PathHints present)
   200→   - **Skip:** `read`, `search`, `task`, `unknown`
   201→   - Deterministic ID: SHA-256 of `sessionID + exchangeID + messageID + path`
   202→   - Maps tool type to change type: `write` → `"write"` (can refine to create/edit later)
   203→   - AgentType from `sessionData.Provider.ID`
   204→   - AgentModel from `message.Model`
   205→   - Note: PathHints are on `Message` (`msg.PathHints`), not on `ToolInfo`
   206→
   207→2. Create `PushAgentEvents(ctx context.Context, engine *provenance.Engine, events []provenance.AgentEvent) []*provenance.ProvenanceRecord`
   208→   - Pushes each event to engine
   209→   - Collects and returns any non-nil ProvenanceRecords
   210→   - Logs each push at DEBUG level, each match at INFO level
   211→
   212→3. Wire into session callback in `main.go`:
   213→   ```go
   214→   if provenanceEngine != nil && session.SessionData != nil {
   215→       events := provenance.ExtractAgentEvents(session.SessionData)
   216→       records := provenance.PushAgentEvents(ctx, provenanceEngine, events)
   217→   }
```
</details></tool-use>

   218→   ```
   219→   - The library handles dedup via `INSERT OR IGNORE`, so pushing the same events on every callback is safe

   197→**Steps:**
   198→
   199→1. Create `pkg/provenance/fswatcher.go`:
   200→   - `FSWatcher` struct wrapping `fsnotify.Watcher` (already a CLI dependency)
   201→   - Watches project directory recursively (`filepath.WalkDir` + add new dirs on Create)
   202→   - On file Create/Write/Remove events, generates `provenance.FileEvent`
   203→   - Pushes to engine via `PushFileEvent`
   204→   - Debounces rapid changes (same file within 100ms)
   205→   - FileEvent details: ID=UUID, Path=absolute, ChangeType=create/modify/delete, Timestamp=file ModTime
   206→
   207→2. Create `pkg/provenance/filter.go`:
   208→   - **Directory exclusions:** `.git`, `.specstory`, `node_modules`, `.next`, `__pycache__`, `.venv`, `vendor`, `.idea`, `.vscode`
   209→   - **File exclusions:** binary extensions, lock files, temp files
   210→   - **Gitignore-style patterns:** Parse `.gitignore` and `.intentignore` from project root
   211→   - `ShouldWatch(path string, isDir bool) bool`
   212→   - **New dependency decision needed:** `sabhiram/go-gitignore` for gitignore parsing
   213→
   214→3. Wire into run/watch commands:
   215→   ```go
   216→   fsWatcher, err := provenance.NewFSWatcher(provenanceEngine, cwd)
   217→   fsWatcher.Start(ctx)
   218→   defer fsWatcher.Stop()
   219→   ```
   220→
